ui_exploration_note: Live server probed READ-ONLY at http://127.0.0.1:3001 — GET /api/health returned status:ok; GET /api/workspaces (no projectId) returned HTTP 400, confirming the project/issue-scoping guard. No workspaces were created, mutated, or agents spawned.

capabilities.name: Workspaces & Worktrees
capabilities.business_purpose: A workspace is the atomic unit of agentic work: it binds one ticket to one isolated place an AI agent can write code (a dedicated git branch in a dedicated worktree, or the main checkout for a 'direct' workspace) plus the bookkeeping that lets the board observe the agent, surface its diff, and decide whether the work is mergeable. It exists so an autonomous agent's blast radius is contained, observable, and reversible. The DB row outlives the process and retains forensic state (launch errors, merged SHAs, cleanup warnings) so finished/crashed/abandoned work is still reasoned about.

actors.name: operator
actors.notes: the single local user, driving via the React UI (New Workspace, View Diff, Merge, chat) — this is single-user local-first, no RBAC

actors.name: monitor
actors.notes: in-process deterministic monitor / out-of-process Conductor — auto-starts unblocked backlog up to WIP, auto-reviews/auto-merges, and triggers the post-merge cascade; gated by per-project Start Mode

actors.name: butler
actors.notes: warm per-project Claude assistant; creates workspaces via the same POST /api/workspaces one-step flow

actors.name: agent-subprocess
actors.notes: the launched AI coding agent (Claude/Codex/Copilot/Pi) that edits code inside the worktree; its writes are confined by a PreToolUse cross-worktree-write gate

actors.name: cli-mcp-caller
actors.notes: pnpm cli / MCP tools driving the same REST surface (e.g. workspace resume)

entry_points.observable: creates DB record + (unless direct) provisions an isolated worktree, moves issue to In Progress, auto-launches the agent subprocess; returns 201 with the workspace record (even on launch failure, with an error field)
dry-run launch config (resolved branch/base/provider/ports, plan-mode default, blockedBy, warnings) with NO side effects
slim scoped workspace list (status/provider/model/mergedAt); 400 when neither scope given
read details / patch status / cascade-delete sessions+messages
send a follow-up message (content field); 201 resumed or ok; 400 when content missing
ETag-cached working-tree diff vs base (200 + ETag, 304 on matching If-None-Match)
lands the branch onto base, sets mergedAt/mergedHeadSha, moves issue to Done; deduped against concurrent calls; 409 on conflict/not-approved/dirty-main/already-merged
conflict path: spawn a fixing agent / rebase onto base / resolve+land / report conflict files
(re)launch agent (201) / stop running session / stop+move issue back to In Progress
plan-mode gate: read the pending plan, approve it to start implementation (201), or reject with feedback (201, 400 if feedback missing)
post-merge cascade: dependents whose blockers are now all Done get a worktree + launched agent automatically
operator hygiene: list/remove orphaned worktree dirs and retry failed post-merge cleanup
operator creates a workspace, sees resolved branch/base/provider and warnings before launch
relaunches the agent for issue N's workspace

behaviors.statement: Creating a workspace provisions an isolated worktree on a fresh branch, injects ticket context, moves the issue to In Progress, and auto-launches the agent in one step
preconditions: no open direct workspace already on the issue
behaviors.observable_outcome: HTTP 201 with the workspace record; issue moves to In Progress; an agent session starts; GET /:id/diff becomes reachable

behaviors.statement: A create request without an issueId is rejected at the boundary — no half-formed work unit may enter the system
behaviors.observable_outcome: HTTP 400, no workspace row created

behaviors.statement: A worktree-provisioning or agent-launch failure still persists the workspace row with an error/latestLaunchError field instead of dropping it, so the failure is visible and recoverable
preconditions: provisioning or launch fails (e.g. stale safety preflight, worktree create error)
behaviors.observable_outcome: Response carries the workspace record plus an error field; workspace later shows as failed/idle; orphaned worktree is rolled back on a DB-txn failure

behaviors.statement: Creation refuses a second open direct workspace for an issue, because two would both edit the shared main checkout and collide
preconditions: an existing non-closed direct workspace on the same issue
behaviors.observable_outcome: create is blocked (error surfaced), no second direct workspace row appears

behaviors.statement: Plan mode auto-enables for a high/critical-priority issue when the caller did not set it explicitly — a cost gate that surfaces an expensive misunderstanding as an approvable plan before tokens are burned; explicit caller intent still wins
behaviors.observable_outcome: preview/create resolves planMode=true; an explicit planMode=false is honored even for high priority

behaviors.statement: Preview returns the fully-resolved launch config (branch, base, provider/profile/model, derived dev ports, plan-mode default, blockedBy, collision/missing-profile/no-base warnings) as a read-only dry run with no side effects
behaviors.observable_outcome: HTTP 200 with the resolved config; no workspace is created; 400 when issueId missing

behaviors.statement: The workspace list is always project- or issue-scoped; an unscoped request is rejected rather than returning the whole table
behaviors.observable_outcome: GET with ?projectId= or ?issueId= returns 200 with the slim scoped list (model column included); without either returns HTTP 400 (confirmed live)

behaviors.statement: A follow-up turn re-engages the agent on an existing workspace by sending a content message, resuming the provider session
behaviors.observable_outcome: 201 with resumed:true + sessionId (or ok:true when delivered to a live session); a new agent run starts against the same worktree

behaviors.statement: A turn request with no content field is rejected
behaviors.observable_outcome: HTTP 400 {error: 'content is required'}, no session started

behaviors.statement: The working-tree diff (including untracked files) is exposed and conditionally cached, so an unchanged diff is a cheap 304 and a changed diff invalidates to a new ETag
preconditions: workspace has a worktree (or is direct → diff vs HEAD)
behaviors.observable_outcome: 200 with ETag + diff body; 304 on matching If-None-Match; new ETag after the diff changes

behaviors.statement: Merging lands the feature branch onto the base, stamps mergedAt + mergedHeadSha (which survive branch deletion), closes the workspace, and moves the issue to Done — terminal and irreversible
preconditions: workspace ready for merge (or auto_merge_in_review bypass)
behaviors.observable_outcome: 200 with merged:true + baseBranch/SHAs; board reflects Done immediately; concurrent merge calls are deduplicated to one promise

behaviors.statement: A merge whose branch conflicts is refused with a structured 409 (reason=conflict + conflictFiles), the readyForMerge flag is cleared, the base is NOT advanced, and the server stays healthy
preconditions: branch conflicts with base (read-only merge-tree detection)
behaviors.observable_outcome: HTTP 409 with {mergeReason:'conflict', conflictFiles}; base branch unchanged; workspace stays open; health endpoint still responsive

behaviors.statement: A merge of a workspace that is not ready for merge is refused unless an auto-merge-in-review bypass is active
behaviors.observable_outcome: HTTP 409 with mergeReason=not_approved; base unchanged

behaviors.statement: A merge is refused when the main checkout has uncommitted tracked source changes, because landing into a dirty main would otherwise stall automation
preconditions: uncommitted tracked .ts/.tsx/.sql changes under packages/** in the main checkout
behaviors.observable_outcome: HTTP 409 with mergeReason=dirty_main; no git merge performed

behaviors.statement: A retry whose branch is already an ancestor of base (e.g. a dropped response after the real merge) reconciles to Done without double-merging — idempotent, git-verified, and guarded against silent-merge-loss when the mergedAt flag is stale
preconditions: branch tip already an ancestor of base OR mergedAt already set AND git-verified ancestor
behaviors.observable_outcome: 200 reconciled:true, mergeBranch NOT called; issue moved to Done; a stale mergedAt with a non-ancestor branch does NOT short-circuit

behaviors.statement: Fix-and-merge spawns a conflict-resolving 'fixing' agent that counts as active capacity, then re-attempts the landing; stale zero-output fixing zombies are force-recovered before retrying
behaviors.observable_outcome: a fixing session starts (consumes a WIP slot); on success the branch lands and the issue moves to Done

behaviors.statement: A plan-mode workspace produces a plan and awaits human approval; the agent writes no implementation code until the plan is approved, and a rejection re-runs planning with feedback
behaviors.observable_outcome: GET /:id/plan returns the plan; POST /:id/implement-plan (201) starts implementation; POST /:id/reject-plan with feedback (201) re-plans; reject without feedback → 400

behaviors.statement: Stopping a workspace halts its running session and resets an active/reviewing workspace to idle (stranded-session recovery); quarantine additionally moves the issue back to In Progress
preconditions: workspace has a running or stranded session
behaviors.observable_outcome: running session stopped; workspace status → idle; quarantine also moves the issue to In Progress; safe (no-op) when nothing is running

behaviors.statement: Deleting a workspace cascade-removes its sessions and messages and detaches its summary from the issue
behaviors.observable_outcome: workspace + all FK children deleted; the issue's workspaceSummary disappears from the board

behaviors.statement: When the agent process exits the run is classified by observed output: a ≤1s / zero-token / explicit-launchFailure run is 'failed' (relaunchable), a clean exit with committed changes flips the workspace to reviewing and moves the issue to In Review, and the exit-before-output drain prevents misclassifying a fast real run as a zero-output launch failure
preconditions: an agent session was launched and the process exits
behaviors.observable_outcome: workspace/session status reflects failed vs idle vs reviewing; on clean exit with a diff the issue is In Review and an auto-review session launches

behaviors.statement: An agent that produces zero output for 15 minutes is killed by a spawn-layer watchdog (reset on every output event), turning an invisible provider deadlock into an observable exit
preconditions: no stdout/stderr for the watchdog interval
behaviors.observable_outcome: the agent process is killed and the session transitions to a stopped/failed state

behaviors.statement: A detached agent survives a server hot-reload: on restart, sessions with a live PID are reattached (output watcher resumes from the last byte offset) and dead-PID sessions are marked stopped with their workspaces reset to idle
behaviors.observable_outcome: surviving agents keep streaming after restart; crashed ones are reconciled to idle; not killed on SIGTERM, only on SIGINT

behaviors.statement: On merge, dependents whose blocking deps are now ALL Done get a worktree created and an agent launched automatically — the dependency DAG drives new work — but only when the project has a defaultBranch, the dependent has no in-flight workspace, and Start Mode permits it
preconditions: dependents exist with all blockers Done
no non-closed workspace already on the dependent
behaviors.observable_outcome: new workspaces appear + agents launch for the unblocked dependents; a workspace_merged event is emitted only after the agent actually launches; skipped when no defaultBranch or dependent already in flight

behaviors.statement: A direct workspace operates in the project's main checkout with no worktree (null baseBranch), trading isolation for immediacy; its merge is a no-op close and its diff is computed against HEAD plus untracked files
behaviors.observable_outcome: no worktree dir; workingDir = repoPath; diff vs HEAD; merge just closes the workspace; setup script skipped

behaviors.statement: Per-worktree dependency bootstrap validates symlink target names against path traversal and absolute paths before creating any link, so autonomous setup cannot write outside the worktree
behaviors.observable_outcome: traversal/absolute link names are rejected; a node_modules request expands to each packages/*/node_modules under the strict pnpm linker

behaviors.statement: Operators can find orphaned worktree directories and retry failed post-merge cleanup, with path-safety guards that reject removal of anything outside the managed .worktrees/ dir
preconditions: orphaned worktree dir or a workspace with a cleanupWarning exists
behaviors.observable_outcome: stale list/cleanup-warnings returned; removal succeeds for safe paths; 400 for unsafe path / traversal / not-closed workspace / repo root

permissions.notes: single-user local-first — the operator can drive every entry point; there is no role/permission matrix in this product
auto-start paths are gated by per-project Start Mode (manual|monitor|conductor) via resolveStartPolicy, NOT by user permissions — a manual project must not cascade/auto-start
a hard PreToolUse gate (prevent-cross-worktree-writes.js) blocks writes resolving into the main checkout or another worktree; this is the runtime containment boundary, not an RBAC role

state_model.notes: The stored `status` column (free text, default 'active') and the DERIVED activity state are two vocabularies: 'merged' (closed+mergedAt), 'in-review-idle' (idle In Review with a cached non-empty diff), and 'failed' (launch-failure heuristic) are DERIVED by deriveWorkspaceActivityState, not stored. 'Active capacity' (consumes a WIP slot) = active|fixing|reviewing|awaiting-plan-approval, but the monitor auto-start gate uses a SEPARATE 3-state list that omits awaiting-plan-approval — the two do not agree.

transitions.trigger: agent exits 0 with committed changes → issue In Review + auto-review launches
review session idles with a cached non-empty diff
POST /:id/merge succeeds → issue Done, mergedAt stamped
DELETE (cascade) or issue moved to a terminal status

error_states.statement: create without issueId → 400
GET /api/workspaces without projectId|issueId → 400 (confirmed live)
POST /:id/turn without content → 400
POST /:id/reject-plan without feedback → 400
merge with conflicting branch → 409 {mergeReason:'conflict', conflictFiles}, base not advanced, server stays healthy
merge when readyForMerge=false and no auto-merge bypass → 409 mergeReason=not_approved
merge with a dirty main checkout → 409 mergeReason=dirty_main
merge of an already-merged workspace → 409 reason=already_merged (or reconcile to Done on retry)
mergeWorkspace WorkspaceError for a stale server build → 503 reason=server_build_stale
GET /api/workspaces/:id for a missing workspace → 404
PATCH /api/workspaces/:id with an invalid status → rejected
stale-worktree/cleanup removal of a path outside .worktrees/ (or traversal/repo-root) → 400
agent run ≤1s / 0 tokens / explicit launchFailure → classified failed (not idle), relaunchable, provider stderr surfaced

unknowns.why_unknown: no plan-mode UI E2E test in the candidate set; not exercised live (would require spawning an agent, which was out of bounds)

unknowns.why_unknown: gating lives upstream in resolveStartPolicy (separate capability); not exercised here and no cascade test in the candidate set

unknowns.why_unknown: heuristic noted in domain doc as overridable via env but not per-provider; not testable without a long-running real agent

unknowns.why_unknown: asserted in unit tests (resolveMergeState direct-close) but not driven through a live merge to avoid mutation

capabilities.name: Issues & Board (kanban core)
capabilities.business_purpose: The work-unit substrate of the whole product: projects (managed git repos), issues/tickets (units of AI coding work), configurable per-project workflow statuses, tags, milestones and typed dependency edges — plus the aggregated, classified board read model that humans (React board) and agents (MCP get_board_status) both observe. Every other capability moves issues across the board or reads its state.

actors.name: operator
actors.notes: single local user driving the React board / REST

actors.name: agent
actors.notes: Butler / MCP / batch-generating agent creating issues + declaring coupling edges by index

actors.name: monitor
actors.notes: in-process monitor / Conductor reading the board read model (blocked map, attention buckets) to decide auto-starts and auto-merges

entry_points.observable: creates one issue (title required); allocates per-project issueNumber=MAX+1; 201 with issueNumber
atomically creates N issues + declared dependency edges (by index) + optional parentIssueId child_of + driveTarget Drive; 201 or 400-with-index, persists nothing on failure
lists a project's issues; slim omits description; statusName filters
updates fields/status; terminal-move guard applies; bulk is atomic
add/remove typed edges; cycle/self/dup/cross-project checked
propose/apply contraction of a coupled component onto a lead
moves Done issues older than N days to Archived; {archived:n}
board read model columns+nested issues; ETag/304 conditional-GET fast path
configure per-project workflow columns; delete blocked while issues linked
manage tags + merge duplicates; builtin tags protected (403)
group issues; progress+burndown summary
assembled classified board overview (effective status, attention, mergeState, totals)

behaviors.statement: Creating an issue requires a non-empty title and allocates a per-project sequential issueNumber (MAX+1), independent across projects and reused after the max is deleted
preconditions: project exists with at least one status
behaviors.observable_outcome: 201 with issueNumber starting at 1 and incrementing per project; whitespace/empty title -> 400; missing projectId/statusId -> 400

behaviors.statement: Batch-create atomically inserts N issues with consecutive numbers plus optional parent_of edges and a Drive record, rolling back entirely (persisting nothing) on any validation failure
behaviors.observable_outcome: 201 with N issues + consecutive issueNumbers; parentIssueId wires child_of edges; driveTarget+parent creates active Drive (no Drive without parent); a bad item -> 400 {index} and zero rows persisted

behaviors.statement: A typed dependency edge can be added singly (POST /:id/dependencies, defaults depends_on) or declared by 0-based index in a batch create/edge batch; symmetric types (related_to/coupled_with) carry no ordering
preconditions: target issues exist in same project
behaviors.observable_outcome: 201 with {id,type}; batch returns {added,skipped}; idempotent re-add skipped; related_to reverse edge allowed

behaviors.statement: Adding a directional edge (depends_on/blocked_by/parent_of/child_of) that would close a cycle is rejected; symmetric pairs are exempt; rollback leaves edges unchanged
behaviors.observable_outcome: single add -> 409 'cycle'; batch -> 400 'cycle' with no edges persisted; transitive A->B->C + C->A also rejected; coupled_with mutual pair accepted

behaviors.statement: Self-edges, unsupported edge types, cross-project edges, and duplicate (issue,dependsOn,type) edges are rejected with typed errors
behaviors.observable_outcome: self -> 400 'itself'; duplicate -> 409 'already exists'; same pair different type allowed; bad type/cross-project -> 400

behaviors.statement: Deleting a dependency edge removes it so it no longer appears in the issue's dependency list
behaviors.observable_outcome: DELETE -> 200 {success:true}; subsequent GET /dependencies omits it

behaviors.statement: Moving an issue to a terminal status (Done/Cancelled) is BLOCKED while it has an open, non-direct, unmerged workspace (AK-535 silent-merge-loss guard); the block is a no-op and the bulk path is atomic
preconditions: issue has an open non-direct workspace
behaviors.observable_outcome: updateIssue/updateIssuesBulk reject with code CONFLICT; status unchanged; workspace not auto-closed; closed/merged or direct or no workspace -> move allowed

behaviors.statement: Transitioning into a terminal status closes any still-open (direct) workspace so the monitor stops relaunching it; non-terminal moves and already-closed workspaces are untouched (mergedAt preserved)
behaviors.observable_outcome: open direct workspace -> status 'closed'; non-terminal change leaves workspace idle; already-closed workspace keeps status+mergedAt

behaviors.statement: An issue's externalUrl must be absent or a well-formed http(s) URL; non-http schemes (javascript:/data:) are rejected to prevent smuggling executable payloads into a new-tab link
behaviors.observable_outcome: valid https persists and round-trips; javascript: -> rejected (4xx); empty/null clears it

behaviors.statement: Contraction collapses a connected coupled_with component onto one lead: external sequential edges are repointed onto the lead (deduped, no self-edges), internal coupling dropped, members become duplicates of the lead and move to the resolved terminal status (Cancelled preferred, else Done)
preconditions: issueIds exactly equal the lead's coupled_with component; no open workspaces; project has a Cancelled or Done status
behaviors.observable_outcome: 200 with rewired edges + members absorbed (description 'Absorbed into #N', status Cancelled); partial selection -> 400 'exactly match'; cross-project -> 400 'across projects'; would-be-cycle -> 400 'cycle' with full rollback

behaviors.statement: getBoardStatus assembles each issue's EFFECTIVE column from its most-relevant workspace's workflow node (falling back to the issue's own statusId), computes totals, and excludes Done/Cancelled unless includeClosed
behaviors.observable_outcome: issues carry derived statusName (workspace node wins over stale issue status), diffStats, lastAgentMessage, totals.inProgress/activeWorkspaces/runningSessions; unknown project -> NotFoundError

behaviors.statement: An In-Review issue is purely classified into an attention bucket (closed-in-review / stale-in-review / idle-awaiting) or a pending_merge bucket (when auto-merge + auto-merge-in-review are enabled and a real diff exists) — one shared policy for human and agent
preconditions: issue in In Review with a workspace
behaviors.observable_outcome: zero-diff not-ready -> attention idle-awaiting; null diff -> stale-in-review; closed workspace -> closed-in-review; idle+real diff+auto-merge -> mergeState pending_merge (attention null); ready-for-merge -> no flags

behaviors.statement: GET /:id/board returns columns whose counts/placements reflect every mutation (PATCH status, workspace merge, workspace delete), with a memoized ETag conditional-GET fast path that serves 304 without recompute until a board-changed broadcast bumps the generation
behaviors.observable_outcome: PATCH status moves issue between columns and updates counts; merge broadcast moves to Done; workspace delete removes workspaceSummary; matching If-None-Match -> 304 with no rebuild; a mutation broadcast or >60s or different includeArchived shape forces a full compute

behaviors.statement: An issue is blocked iff it has a depends_on/blocked_by edge to a not-yet-resolved blocker; 'resolved' uses the WIDER {Done, AI Reviewed, Cancelled} set, but a blocker only truly unblocks once it has also landed on base (mergedAt / isDirect / no workspace); fan-in dependents stay blocked until ALL blockers land
behaviors.observable_outcome: monitor selects a Todo issue only when every blocker is resolved+landed; AI-Reviewed blocker counts resolved; a missing blocker is conservatively still-blocking; cycle members are not selected

behaviors.statement: archive-done moves issues that are Done and whose statusChangedAt is strictly older than N days into the Archived column
preconditions: project has Done and Archived statuses
behaviors.observable_outcome: issues strictly older than the cutoff move to Archived (boundary: exactly-at-cutoff stays Done); {archived:n}; invalid olderThanDays -> 400

behaviors.statement: Listing returns a project's issues with their per-project issueNumbers; the slim variant omits descriptions and statusName filters to one column
behaviors.observable_outcome: GET without projectId -> 400; with projectId -> issues array; statusName=Nope -> []; slim omits description (~60% payload)

behaviors.statement: Per-project workflow columns are CRUD-configurable; a status that still has linked issues cannot be deleted (would leave issues dangling with no fallback column)
behaviors.observable_outcome: GET lists >=5 default statuses; POST creates a new column; DELETE of a status with linked issues -> 409 (no orphaned issues)

behaviors.statement: Tags are CRUD-managed and duplicates can be merged; built-in tags are protected (cannot be deleted/renamed/merged away) and deleting a tag detaches it from issues without deleting the issues
behaviors.observable_outcome: duplicate name -> 409; builtin delete/rename/merge -> 403; deleting a tag removes its issue associations only; non-builtin tags delete/rename normally

behaviors.statement: Milestones group issues toward a deliverable; the summary aggregates per-milestone totals (total/open/closed/progressPercent) and a server-computed burndown series
behaviors.observable_outcome: summary returns per-milestone counts, progressPercent, and burndown of requested length; empty milestone reports zeros with a full-length burndown

behaviors.statement: Project registration seeds a fixed canonical 7-status workflow (Backlog at sortOrder -1, Todo as the single default landing column, plus In Progress/In Review/AI Reviewed/Done/Cancelled)
behaviors.observable_outcome: initializeProjectStatuses produces exactly the 7 canonical names, no duplicates, Backlog sortOrder -1, exactly one default (Todo)

permissions.notes: single-user local app — no RBAC; the only enforced 'permission'-like rules are builtin-tag protection (403) and the status-delete-with-issues guard (409), both modeled as error-handling, not roles

state_model.notes: Per-project, reorderable; the diagram is the seeded default. Effective state is DERIVED from the most-relevant workspace's workflow node, with the issue's statusId as fallback.

transitions.trigger: terminal move (BLOCKED while open non-direct unmerged workspace)

error_states.statement: empty/whitespace title -> 400
directional cycle -> 409 (single) / 400 (batch)
duplicate edge -> 409 'already exists'
terminal move with open non-direct workspace -> CONFLICT
non-http(s) externalUrl rejected
contraction with no Cancelled/Done status -> BAD_REQUEST
delete a status with linked issues -> 409
delete/rename/merge-away a builtin tag -> 403

external_integrations: git CLI (diff/conflict cache feeding board read model)

unknowns.why_unknown: no concurrency test forces a real collision; behavior asserted only sequentially

unknowns.question: Does the contraction-requires-terminal-status BAD_REQUEST path have a test?
unknowns.why_unknown: happy/partial/cross-project/cycle paths are tested; the 'no Cancelled/Done status' rejection was not observed in the candidate set

unknowns.why_unknown: domain doc flags it; no test renames Done to confirm the legacy fallback loses terminal semantics

capabilities.business_purpose: The anti-corruption seam between the board's uniform 'launch an agent on this ticket' notion and four mutually-incompatible third-party coding CLIs (Anthropic claude, OpenAI codex, GitHub copilot, pi). It translates provider-neutral launch options into a concrete spawn {command,args,env}, normalizes each CLI's stdout into one ParsedStreamEvent model, validates that a chosen provider+profile can actually launch (auth/binary/version preflight), bootstraps OAuth logins, and rotates credential rings on rate-limit exhaustion. Without it the board could track tickets but spawn no agent.

actors.name: agent-sessions
actors.notes: primary consumer: agent.service.ts builds a launch and spawns the CLI

actors.name: operator
actors.notes: single local user; selects provider/profile/model in Settings, triggers OAuth login, views the profile-health dashboard

actors.name: monitor
actors.notes: Strategy Bullseye + selectProviderFromStrategy feed the default provider; usage-limit exit dispatcher drives ring rotation

actors.name: agent-cli
actors.notes: the four third-party CLIs whose unversioned flag/wire contracts this module shields against

entry_points.observable: returns a {command,args,useShell,env,stdin-hints} spawn recipe for the named provider
coerces any untrusted string to a canonical ProviderName, defaulting to claude
maps a provider to its profile-pref key (codex_profile, pi_profile, ...)
normalizes a provider stdout line to ParsedStreamEvent; loud-logs valid-JSON-unrecognized lines under the canonical provider name
computes ok/warning/error verdict per (provider,profile) for the Settings dashboard
probes <cli> --version and range-checks it (below-min=error, above/unparseable/unavailable=warning)
pops a real visible terminal running claude /login | codex login with the right CONFIG_DIR/CODEX_HOME
reports whether the global provider/profile prefs diverge from the Strategy Bullseye (#903)
on a usage-limit session exit: cools the exhausted profile, rotates the credential ring, relaunches a builder on the fresh profile or blocks

behaviors.statement: buildAgentLaunchConfig dispatches by ProviderName to the matching adapter and returns a ready-to-spawn {command,args,env} plus stdin-delivery hints
preconditions: a ProviderName (or legacy id) is chosen
behaviors.observable_outcome: config.command/args/env reflect the provider's invocation grammar; claude-code routes to claude; unknown provider throws in registry but narrows to claude at narrowProviderName

behaviors.statement: Any untrusted provider string (pref/request/legacy claude-code) narrows to a canonical ProviderName and defaults to claude rather than throwing
preconditions: a stored or request provider string exists
behaviors.observable_outcome: narrowProviderName returns one of claude/codex/copilot/pi; claude-code→claude; junk→claude; getProfilePrefKey returns the matching pref key

behaviors.statement: For a Claude profile that sets a custom ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL (e.g. z.ai/glm), --model is omitted so the profile's own ANTHROPIC_MODEL decides the model
preconditions: claudeProfile settings.json defines a custom ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL
behaviors.observable_outcome: the launch config contains no --model flag for custom-endpoint profiles but does for standard profiles

behaviors.statement: A provider-agnostic default_model id is dropped unless it belongs to the chosen provider's family, preventing a stale gpt-* from killing a Claude launch (and vice versa); unknown ids pass through; copilot/pi never strip
preconditions: a model id is present from a provider-scoped pref or request
behaviors.observable_outcome: modelBelongsToProvider returns false (→ model dropped) for cross-family ids, true for matching/unknown/copilot/pi

behaviors.statement: Codex exec flags (--json, sandbox, --profile, --model) are emitted BEFORE the resume subcommand because codex exec resume rejects them after resume and exits code 2
behaviors.observable_outcome: in the codex args array all exec options precede the literal 'resume' token

behaviors.statement: Plan ('produce a plan, change nothing') mode is enforced per provider by the strongest mechanism — Claude --permission-mode plan, Codex read-only sandbox + prompt prefix, Copilot --plan + --deny-tool list, Pi prompt prefix — and non-Claude plans are wrapped in ===PLAN BEGIN/END=== markers
behaviors.observable_outcome: each provider's config carries its plan enforcement (flag/sandbox/deny-tool/prefix) and emits the agreed plan markers where applicable

behaviors.statement: Each provider adapter normalizes its CLI's stdout JSON into one ParsedStreamEvent model (providerSessionId, liveStats, stats, toolActivity, todos, turnComplete, rate-limit)
preconditions: a stdout line is read from a running agent
behaviors.observable_outcome: parseStreamEvent returns the unified event fields for valid lines and undefined for non-JSON/fieldless lines, per claude/codex/copilot/pi wire format

behaviors.statement: A valid-JSON-but-unrecognized stream line is loud-logged via parseStreamEventObserved under the canonical provider name instead of silently swallowed (the #898 0-tokens misdiagnosis guard)
preconditions: a recognized CLI emits an unknown/renamed event shape
behaviors.observable_outcome: the unknown event is recorded under its canonical provider name; a recognized event (e.g. codex turn.completed) is NOT counted as unknown

behaviors.statement: One-shot text mode produces a non-streaming launch for internal AI utilities — Claude --output-format text -p, Codex exec WITHOUT --json reading prompt from stdin — so a text utility never sends Claude flags to codex
behaviors.observable_outcome: the config emits plain-text args (no stream-json/MCP) and the correct per-provider non-streaming flags

behaviors.statement: A CLI --version probe is range-checked: below the supported floor is a hard error, above-known/unparseable/unavailable are non-blocking warnings, and the probe is cached once per (provider,command)
preconditions: a provider command resolvable on PATH
behaviors.observable_outcome: detectCliVersion returns below-min→error, in-range→ok, no-semver→unparseable warning, throw/missing→unavailable warning, exceeds maxKnown→above-known warning

behaviors.statement: listAgentProfileHealth computes an ok/warning/error verdict per (provider,profile) from auth/config existence + binary-on-PATH + buildable flags + version probe, and a recorded launch FAILURE overrides an otherwise-ok static verdict to error
preconditions: Settings opened or a profile selected
behaviors.observable_outcome: the dashboard shows error for a missing codex config / below-min version / recorded launch failure; secrets in flags/errors are redacted

behaviors.statement: Provider/profile/model resolution funnels through one shared resolver with fixed precedence: explicit profileOverride > legacy claudeProfile > Strategy selection > provider pref; the model is read ONLY from provider-scoped keys (the global default_model is unrepresentable, #902)
preconditions: a workspace launch or preview is requested
behaviors.observable_outcome: resolveProviderConfig returns the right provider/profile per precedence; a stale global default_model never leaks into a launch; copilot never gets a model

behaviors.statement: The board reports when the global provider/profile prefs diverge from the Strategy Bullseye selection so the operator can re-sync them (#903 anti-drift)
preconditions: a project may or may not have a Bullseye configured
behaviors.observable_outcome: GET /api/preferences/provider-divergence returns hasBullseye + diverged; diverged=true on provider or profile mismatch, false when they agree or no Bullseye

behaviors.statement: On a usage-limit session exit the exhausted profile is cooled and the provider's credential ring rotated to the next usable profile BEFORE any relaunch; the worktree is auto-relaunched on the fresh profile only if rotation succeeded AND it was a builder session, otherwise the workspace is left blocked with a human-readable reason
preconditions: session stats match a codex/claude usage-limit signature
behaviors.observable_outcome: ring picks next non-cooled license/subscription, switches the active-profile pref, builder resumes same worktree with a continuation prompt; non-builder or no-fresh-profile → blocked, never silently relaunched

behaviors.statement: Picking a Codex license / Claude subscription with no auth on disk creates the credential dir and pops a REAL visible terminal (windowsHide:false) running codex login / claude /login with the right CODEX_HOME/CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR; failure is non-fatal because the equivalent manual command is returned for a copy-button
behaviors.observable_outcome: a foreground terminal opens for the OAuth callback server; the manual command string is returned; a hidden/background spawn would tear down the callback

behaviors.statement: Before a profile is applied, the server's own profile-owned ANTHROPIC_* env vars are stripped from the spawn env to prevent cross-profile credential bleed, and if a Claude profile sets ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN but no key, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is deleted so the profile's auth wins cleanly
preconditions: a profile is selected for a Claude launch
behaviors.observable_outcome: the spawn env contains no leaked server ANTHROPIC_* vars; token+key conflict is resolved by dropping the key

permissions.notes: single-user local app — no RBAC; permission dimension is trivial here

transitions.trigger: usage-limit exit, ring rotated to fresh profile, builder session
usage-limit exit, no fresh profile OR non-builder session

error_states.statement: a CLI older than the supported floor yields a hard error verdict in preflight
a recorded launch failure forces a profile's health to error even when static preflight is ok
when every profile is cooling (no rotation) the workspace is left blocked, never silently relaunched
getProvider throws on an unknown provider name (callers must narrow first)

capabilities.name: agent-providers

capabilities.business_purpose: Turns a launched coding-agent subprocess's opaque provider stream into a first-class, observable domain object: live-streams what the agent is doing, classifies HOW the run ended (stopped / usage-limit / launch-failure / completed), persists a transcript + cost/friction stats, and emits the exit verdict that drives the workspace's next state and the autonomous monitor loop. It is the feedback channel of the whole agentic system.

actors.name: operator
actors.notes: single local user — starts/stops sessions, sends follow-up turns, reads transcript/stats via UI

actors.name: monitor
actors.notes: in-process monitor / out-of-process Conductor — auto-starts sessions and consumes exit verdicts to start/merge/unstick tickets

actors.name: agent-subprocess
actors.notes: the spawned provider CLI (claude/codex/copilot/pi) — emits the stream this module parses; survives server hot-reload (detached)

actors.name: board-ui
actors.notes: WebSocket + REST consumer rendering live transcript, todos, stats, and reading history

entry_points.observable: inserts a running session row, launches the agent in the workspace dir, wires streaming + exit; returns sessionId
stop, follow-up turn, recover, re-bind sessions
subscribe to one session's live message stream with full buffer replay on connect
project-wide live invalidation + activity/stats/todos/approval pushes
ETag-cached transcript, persisted stats, server-parsed summary, LIKE transcript search
transcript fetch by board session id; global transcript search filtered by issue
6h background prune of session_messages for old workspaces + per-session row cap

behaviors.statement: Starting a session persists a running session row (with the workspace's skill snapshot) and launches the agent process in the workspace's working directory
behaviors.observable_outcome: sessions row status=running, workspaceId set, skillId/skillName snapshotted; agent launched with workingDir

behaviors.statement: Launch is gated by safety policy: a Codex builder refuses the gpt-5.3-codex-spark model, an absent model defaults to gpt-5.5, a cross-provider stored model is dropped, and a failed workspace preflight blocks the spawn
behaviors.observable_outcome: spark launch rejected (CONFLICT/UNSAFE_CODEX_MODEL, no session row); default resolvedModel=gpt-5.5 in launch stats; cross-provider model not passed as --model; stale-safety preflight throws CONFLICT/STALE_SAFETY_POLICY with no launch

behaviors.statement: The pure exit classifier returns a verdict by strict priority stopped > usage-limit > launch-failure > completed, with a 10s launch-failure window splitting zero-output vs non-zero-error fast exits
preconditions: session exit context built after output drain
behaviors.observable_outcome: classifySessionExit phase + effectiveExitCode/errorText match the priority and window rules at the boundary

behaviors.statement: A clean substantive exit finalizes the session completed, advances the workspace (idle, issue→In Review), writes HANDOFF.md, and fires onSessionExit so the board advances
behaviors.observable_outcome: session status=completed exitCode=0 endedAt set; workspace status=idle; issue moved to In Review; board broadcast workspace_idle/issue_updated; onSessionExit(workspaceId,sessionId,0) called once

behaviors.statement: A fast zero-output exit, or a fast non-zero exit with error text, is finalized as a launch failure: session stopped (exitCode 1, launchFailure stats) and workspace returned to idle/retryable
preconditions: exit within 10s window with zero substantive output OR non-zero exit + error text
behaviors.observable_outcome: session status=stopped exitCode=1; stats.launchFailure=true success=false agentSummary quotes the error; workspace status=idle; onSessionExit(...,1)

behaviors.statement: A provider usage-limit exit is finalized with rate-limit stats and the workspace is routed to blocked (for credential rotation), not idle
preconditions: exit output contains a recognized usage-limit message
behaviors.observable_outcome: stats.rateLimited=true, rateLimitKind, retryAfter, failureReason set; workspace status=blocked

behaviors.statement: Session exit is finalized exactly once even when the exit event fires multiple times (live exit + reattach PID-poll) — no double DB write, no double workflow callback
behaviors.observable_outcome: onSessionExit + workspace-completion workflow run once despite repeated exit callbacks; session row written once

behaviors.statement: A user stop is the authoritative verdict: the session is recorded stopped, the exit handler must not overwrite it, and a repeated stop is a safe no-op
behaviors.observable_outcome: stopSession writes status=stopped; a later exit keeps stopped; second stopSession resolves true with no duplicate side effect; classifier routes stoppedByUser to 'stopped' over every other condition

behaviors.statement: A plan-mode run ALWAYS clears planMode on exit and lands in a visible state: plan recovered from the raw ===PLAN BEGIN/END=== buffer → PLAN.md + auto-continue, or park at awaiting-plan-approval, or no-plan → blocked
behaviors.observable_outcome: workspace.planMode=false in all three cases; PLAN.md written + implement session launched (auto-continue), OR status=awaiting-plan-approval+pendingPlanPath, OR status=blocked with no implement launch

behaviors.statement: The detached-agent output file is drained to EOF before the exit event is emitted, so a crash within one poll interval is not misclassified as a zero-output launch failure (#909)
preconditions: output written between last poll and exit
behaviors.observable_outcome: the tail stdout event arrives before the exit event and is not duplicated with already-polled content

behaviors.statement: Each provider stream chunk is parsed into a normalized display event; real assistant/tool/stats output marks the session substantive while keepalive/error noise does not; a valid-JSON event of unknown type is counted and logged loudly, never silently dropped
behaviors.observable_outcome: display events normalized (init/assistant/tool_use/result/raw); sessionSubstantiveOutput set only on content-bearing events; unknown-event counter increments + warning logged

behaviors.statement: stdout is persisted only to the on-disk .out file while non-stdout events are batched (50 rows / 250ms) into session_messages and flushed on exit; a FK failure from a racing workspace cleanup is swallowed
behaviors.observable_outcome: stdout produces zero DB rows; a 50-event non-stdout batch flushes as one insert; remaining buffer flushed at exit; FK-violation insert does not surface as an error

behaviors.statement: A persisted transcript is parsed server-side (no LLM) into a structured summary — files read/edited/written, commands, errors, model, rate-limits, tool fail-rate friction — uniformly across Claude/Codex/Copilot formats
behaviors.observable_outcome: summary lists files/commands/toolUsePatterns(count,failedCount)/model/rateLimit for each provider format

behaviors.statement: REST read endpoints expose the session: output is ETag-cached (304 on match), plus stats, parsed summary, and LIKE transcript search joined up the issue→project→status chain
behaviors.observable_outcome: GET output returns 200+ETag then 304 on matching If-None-Match; stats/summary return structured bodies; search returns matching transcripts filtered by issue

behaviors.statement: A WebSocket subscriber receives the live message stream, a late subscriber replays the full in-memory buffer on connect, and the buffer is freed only when the last subscriber leaves AND the session has exited
behaviors.observable_outcome: connecting WS receives prior AgentOutputMessage frames (reconnect/replay) then live frames; buffer not freed while a subscriber is attached or the session is live

behaviors.statement: After a server restart, surviving detached agents are reattached (in-memory context/provider restored) and a PID-poll-detected exit mirrors the normal exit path idempotently; a stale running session whose PID is dead is finalized stopped and its workspace set idle
preconditions: a detached session that survived hot-reload, or a dead-PID running session
behaviors.observable_outcome: reattached session resumes broadcast + exit handling; notifyExternalExit finalizes once; cleanupStaleSession sets session=stopped(endedAt) + workspace=idle and purges in-memory state

behaviors.statement: A follow-up turn is gated on turnState==waiting AND a verified-alive process; a dead process returns stale:true so the caller resumes into a fresh session; turnState flips to waiting only on a provider turnComplete with stdin still open
preconditions: an in-flight or just-completed multi-turn session
behaviors.observable_outcome: sendTurn on a waiting+alive session proceeds (state→processing); on a dead/unknown session returns stale; turnState transitions processing↔waiting on result/sendTurn events

behaviors.statement: The provider's own resume token (Claude session_id from system/init, Pi session header) is captured from the stream and stored, then passed as --resume / --session on relaunch so the conversation continues
preconditions: a session that emitted an init/session header
behaviors.observable_outcome: sessions.providerSessionId persisted; a relaunch spawns with --resume <id> and the agent continues the prior conversation

behaviors.statement: A background pruner bounds DB growth: it deletes session_messages for workspaces merged/closed >3 days ago and caps each still-active session at 2000 rows, dropping the oldest excess
preconditions: old merged workspaces / sessions exceeding the row cap
behaviors.observable_outcome: messages for stale merged workspaces removed; per-session rows capped at MAX_MESSAGES_PER_ACTIVE_SESSION

behaviors.statement: Project-scoped change reasons (board_changed, workspace_idle, session_completed, issue_updated …) are broadcast to every board WebSocket subscriber so all views refresh
behaviors.observable_outcome: all subscribers receive the typed event frame on a change

permissions.notes: single-user local app — no RBAC; permission dimension is N/A (one human actor)

state_model.session_status_note: sessions.status ∈ {running, completed, stopped}; the launch-failure and usage-limit verdicts both land on 'stopped' with distinguishing stats (launchFailure / rateLimited).

transitions.trigger: fast zero-output / non-zero error exit (launch failure)

error_states.statement: Codex builder launch with gpt-5.3-codex-spark rejected (CONFLICT/UNSAFE_CODEX_MODEL), no session row created
Stale workspace safety policy blocks launch (CONFLICT/STALE_SAFETY_POLICY) before spawn
A throw during launch records a profile launch-failure, cleans zombie state, marks the session stopped, and rethrows
sendTurn to a dead/unknown session returns stale:true instead of enqueuing a turn
A valid-JSON event of an unknown type is counted + logged loudly, not dropped
A FK-constraint failure on message insert (session deleted by racing cleanup) is swallowed, not logged as error

external_integrations: git CLI (workspace-side collaborators on completed path)
filesystem (.out transcript file, PLAN.md / HANDOFF.md writes)

unknowns.why_unknown: e2e asserts replay but not the free condition

capabilities.name: agent-sessions

capabilities.business_purpose: The product surface of agentic-kanban: the single screen a local operator uses to watch and steer a fleet of AI coding agents. It renders the kanban board (columns = workflow statuses, cards = tickets), lets the user create/move/edit/delete tickets by drag or keyboard, opens issue-detail and workspace/diff panels, exposes a command palette and keyboard shortcuts, and reflects live agent activity (which ticket an agent works, review/merge state, approvals) in real time without manual refresh. It is a mostly-thin optimistic projection of server truth with a few client-local UX policies (WIP visual tint, drop-position arithmetic, archive-confirm gate, column partitioning).

actors.notes: the single local user; drives every UI control

actors.name: server-push

behaviors.observable_outcome: panel shows all fields; in-progress input carries over; Start Workspace reveals sub-options; submit creates; backdrop/Escape/Cancel close it; submit disabled when title empty

behaviors.statement: Clicking a card opens the issue detail panel; the operator can edit and delete the ticket, and Escape/expand-collapse control the panel layout
behaviors.observable_outcome: detail panel opens with ticket data; edits persist; delete removes the card; Escape closes; expand cycles sidebar→modal→fullscreen and collapse reverses

behaviors.statement: Board state changed server-side (agent/monitor/MCP/CLI/API) is reflected in the live UI without a manual refresh, via the WebSocket push + coalesced refetch
preconditions: a server-side issue create or status change
behaviors.observable_outcome: a ticket created via API appears on the board within seconds; a status change relocates the card to the new column

behaviors.statement: A card surfaces its workspace's live agent state — Agent working / AI reviewing / AI fixing — and shows none when idle or workspace-less

behaviors.observable_outcome: the matching activity badge renders for active/reviewing/resolving states; no badge for idle or no-workspace

behaviors.statement: A column over its configured WIP limit tints its header red (advisory only — the move is never blocked); an unset/garbage limit is treated as no limit

behaviors.observable_outcome: column classified under/at/over drives the header tint; zero/negative/non-numeric coerced to no-limit

behaviors.statement: The 'AI Reviewed' staging column renders only when it holds issues, or when auto-review is on without auto-merge; it appears/disappears as tickets enter/leave
behaviors.observable_outcome: column hidden when empty; appears when an issue moves in (with an awaiting-manual-merge hint); hides again when the issue leaves; always shown under autoReview&&!autoMerge

behaviors.statement: The operator switches between Board, Table, and Graph views; each renders its expected content and supports its own interactions (table sort/filter/row-open; graph zoom/legend/status-filter)

behaviors.statement: Ctrl-clicking multiple cards multi-selects them and a bulk action (e.g. priority change) applies to the whole selection

behaviors.statement: Workspace actions (Review/Merge) surface in the palette only for eligible workspaces and are gated by workspace state

behaviors.statement: Single-key shortcuts open panels/overlays (?, a, q) and a context-aware shortcut-help overlay, while being suppressed during text entry

behaviors.statement: Arrow/vim keys move a card cursor across columns and rows, seeding onto the first card and clamping at edges, skipping empty columns

behaviors.statement: The diff viewer shows a workspace's changes (stats header, file tree, added lines, unified/split toggle) and supports full diff-comment CRUD
preconditions: a workspace with a real diff
behaviors.observable_outcome: diff stats + file tree + added lines render; unified/split toggles; comments can be created/edited/deleted via UI; comment count badge updates

behaviors.statement: The board stats bar shows per-status ticket counts and a commits counter, and a Blocked filter narrows the board to blocked issues
behaviors.observable_outcome: stats bar shows counts per status + commits; Blocked filter shows only blocked issues and toggling off restores all

behaviors.statement: The settings panel saves agent/workflow preferences that persist across reopen (agent command, profile, auto-review/auto-fix/auto-merge/board-monitoring toggles)
behaviors.observable_outcome: saved values are present on reopen; workflow toggles persist; cancel closes without saving; profile dropdown exposes harness-named options

Unlike fire-and-forget Builders, it keeps one live in-process LLM session per (project, butler) so conversation context survives across turns AND server restarts (persisted resume id).

behaviors.statement: Starting the butler provisions a warm in-process LLM session for the (project, butler); a second ensure returns the existing session (no context fork)
behaviors.observable_outcome: POST /ensure returns 201 {active:true, sessionId}; GET /butler then shows active:true; cold session starts WITHOUT a resume when no id persisted

behaviors.statement: Sending a message pushes a turn into the warm session and streams an assistant reply back (token deltas + tool cards) over SSE
behaviors.observable_outcome: user bubble appears, mock/assistant reply streams into chat; /message accepted; reply text returned

behaviors.statement: A synchronous /ask turn lets a separate process (CLI/MCP) get a buffered answer without reading the in-memory SSE stream
behaviors.observable_outcome: POST /ask returns {isError:false, text} containing the assistant reply; resolves on result/error or 120s timeout

behaviors.statement: Conversation context persists across turns: the transcript accumulates user+assistant messages and multiple turns keep prior history
behaviors.observable_outcome: GET /messages returns ordered user+assistant messages; two turns yield 4 messages with both prompts present

behaviors.statement: Clearing context (DELETE /butler) stops the session, forgets the resume id, and empties the transcript — the mechanism for 'new session' / applying an edited skill
behaviors.observable_outcome: GET /butler shows active:false; GET /messages returns []; UI shows 'Butler is ready.' placeholder

behaviors.statement: Interrupting cancels the IN-FLIGHT turn (query.interrupt) while keeping the session warm
behaviors.observable_outcome: in-flight stream stops; GET /butler still active:true; next turn accepted

behaviors.statement: Switching model applies live via the SDK setModel control request and PRESERVES conversation context (no restart); the model lives on the global butler definition
behaviors.observable_outcome: POST /model → {ok, model, applied:true}; SDK setModel called with the alias; GET /butler reflects new model; sessionId unchanged

behaviors.statement: Switching profile (auth endpoint) RESTARTS the session and forgets the old resume id — a different endpoint cannot resume the same transcript
behaviors.observable_outcome: POST /profile → {ok, profile, active:true}; a fresh SDK query starts WITHOUT resume; persisted session-id pref now tracks the new session

behaviors.statement: A persisted SDK session id is replayed as resume on next ensure, so the butler survives a server restart with its conversation intact
preconditions: a session id persisted to butler_session_<projectId>
behaviors.observable_outcome: cold GET /butler reports the persisted sessionId; ensure passes options.resume = that id instead of starting a new conversation

behaviors.statement: A dead/unresumable session (Anthropic invalid-thinking-signature 400, or codex 'no rollout found') is recovered: drop the resume, start fresh, and re-send the in-flight turn so the user's message is not lost
behaviors.observable_outcome: first query throws → second query starts WITHOUT resume; session adopts the fresh id; turn succeeds; no error bubble; genuine (non-stale) failures still surface and do NOT retry

behaviors.statement: A turn is rejected while the butler is busy — a single in-flight turn per session, no concurrent streams into one context
behaviors.observable_outcome: POST /message returns 409 (HTTP); at the service layer sendButlerTurn returns false for the second concurrent turn

behaviors.statement: Named butler personas are GLOBAL (shared across projects), CRUD-managed and capped at MAX_BUTLERS=4; the 'default' butler can be renamed/re-modelled but never deleted (it holds back-compat pref keys)
preconditions: none for list; <4 defs to create
behaviors.observable_outcome: POST creates (201) and appears in list; PUT renames; DELETE removes a named one; default always present; a 5th create is refused; deleting default is refused

behaviors.statement: The butler resolves its backend (claude SDK / codex CLI / mock) via the shared provider cascade; a codex license-profile butler launches under its own CODEX_HOME with --profile dropped
behaviors.observable_outcome: GET /butler reports backend matching the provider; GET /profiles lists provider profiles + selected; a codex license butler spawns with CODEX_HOME=licenseDir and no --profile; claude model aliases not applied to a codex butler

behaviors.statement: A project-scoped butler behavior prompt override can be set and retrieved; it wins over the global default and is scoped per project (no leak); deleting reverts to the global
behaviors.observable_outcome: PUT /skill stores prompt; GET /skill returns it with isOverride:true; another project keeps its own; empty PUT reverts to global default

behaviors.statement: The pure client reducer folds the SSE ButlerEvent stream into chat bubbles + collapsible tool cards deterministically (dedupes user echoes, accumulates assistant deltas, settles tool results, never double-renders streamed text)
behaviors.observable_outcome: given a sequence of events the chat state contains the expected bubbles/tool cards; input state is never mutated

behaviors.statement: Slash-command autocomplete: typing '/' offers commands merged from the live SDK supportedCommands and the repo's own .claude/skills, parsed/filtered/applied client-side
behaviors.observable_outcome: GET /commands returns the merged deduped command list; client parse/filter/apply transform the input correctly

behaviors.statement: Board events (merge failures, agent crashes, stuck workspaces) are injected as [system event] turns into the warm DEFAULT butler only, rate-limited to 1 turn / 30s / project with bursts collapsed to a summary line; dropped silently if no session is warm
behaviors.observable_outcome: a [system event] turn appears in the default butler's transcript; named butlers never receive it; ≥2 events in 30s collapse to one 'Nx kind' summary; cold session → event dropped

behaviors.statement: Past-session history is browsable (capped at 50 ids, most-recent-first) and a past session's transcript is readable ONLY if its id is allowlisted to this project's tracked ids (security boundary)
behaviors.observable_outcome: GET /sessions returns the capped list; GET /sessions/:sid/messages returns the transcript for an own id but REFUSES a session id belonging to another project

behaviors.statement: The 'Chat about this ticket' retrospective builds an opening prompt referencing the ticket by number (or title), its spec questions, type/status, and a truncated description
behaviors.observable_outcome: the generated prompt contains the #number, retrospective questions, metadata, and a truncated description (omitted when empty)

permissions.notes: single-user local app — no RBAC; permission axis is near-trivial EXCEPT the cross-project session-transcript allowlist (butler.history.access), which is a real security boundary worth a deny test

error_states.statement: follow-up turn while busy returns 409
invalid-thinking-signature/no-rollout resume recovered to a fresh session; genuine failures surfaced and not retried
synchronous /ask resolves with an error after a 120s timeout
reading a past-session transcript for a session id not allowlisted to this project is refused

external_integrations: codex CLI subprocess + CODEX_HOME license ring

unknowns.why_unknown: not asserted by any test; not probed live to avoid spawning a session

unknowns.why_unknown: no test creates a 5th or deletes default; not probed live

unknowns.why_unknown: no test imports/exercises emitButlerSystemEvent's injection path; usage-limit-rotation-exit only imports the module for an unrelated builder-rotation test

unknowns.why_unknown: no negative-path test; not probed live

capabilities.name: butler

capabilities.business_purpose: The Codemod Factory lets the single local operator state a repo-wide structural refactor in plain English, have an LLM compile it into a precise ts-morph AST transform, see a per-file diff across the whole project as a dry-run, then apply only the chosen files behind a hard write-confinement boundary. It is the board's only mechanism for human-gated, project-wide mechanical edits that bypass the per-ticket agent/worktree pipeline. The design's whole point is a trustworthy preview->apply gate over a destructive, blast-radius operation (rewrite every TS file) with the safety interlocks visible.

actors.name: operator
actors.notes: single local user driving the CodemodPanel overlay (x key / command palette) or hitting the HTTP API directly

actors.name: llm
actors.notes: claude-cli invokeClaudePrompt compiles intent->ts-morph transform body; not an external caller, an internal compiler step

entry_points.observable: compiles intent->transform (or runs a supplied script), dry-runs over all TS files, returns {script, files:[{filePath,relativePath,diff,original,modified}], totalTsFiles, limitReached}; writes nothing
writes the supplied modified content for selected in-repo files; returns {applied[], skipped[]}; rejects any path outside repoPath
lists saved codemods (agent_skills filtered to type==='codemod') for project + global
saves {name,description,script} as an agent_skills row patched to type='codemod'; 201, or 409 on name collision in scope
fetches one saved codemod or 404
full describe->preview->select-files->apply->save workflow; stats bar, collapsible generated script, colorized per-file diff checkbox list

behaviors.statement: Previewing an intent compiles it into a ts-morph transform and returns dry-run per-file diffs across the whole project without writing anything
preconditions: project registered with a resolvable repoPath
LLM (claude-cli) reachable, OR a pre-existing script supplied to skip generation
behaviors.observable_outcome: 200 with a generated script string, a files[] of changed files each carrying unified diff + original/modified, totalTsFiles count, limitReached flag; the project's files on disk are unchanged

behaviors.statement: Preview refuses without the two required inputs (description and projectId), returning a 400 that names the missing field
behaviors.observable_outcome: 400 with error matching /description/i when description absent; 400 with error matching /projectId/i when projectId absent

behaviors.statement: A project with more than 100 TS files refuses to preview until the caller explicitly acknowledges the blast radius via overrideLimit:true
behaviors.observable_outcome: ValidationError surfaced instructing the caller to resend overrideLimit:true (the UI shows a yellow 'run on all files' override); resending with overrideLimit:true proceeds to diff

behaviors.statement: Apply confines every write to the project repo: a target path that resolves outside repoPath is rejected and the file on disk is left untouched (the core security boundary)
preconditions: a change whose filePath is outside repoPath (foreign absolute or .. traversal)
behaviors.observable_outcome: 400 with error matching /outside the project/i; the out-of-repo file's content is unchanged

behaviors.statement: Apply writes the supplied modified content to disk for the selected in-repo files (empty selection = all changed files) and reports applied vs skipped
behaviors.observable_outcome: 200 with {applied[], skipped[]}; the named files now contain the modified text; applied contains the written path and skipped is empty when all selected

behaviors.statement: Apply refuses without projectId (the security boundary) or with an empty changes array, returning 400
behaviors.observable_outcome: 400 with error matching /projectId/i when projectId absent; 400 when changes is missing/empty

behaviors.statement: A working transform is saved as a named reusable codemod (agent_skills row tagged type='codemod') and appears in the codemod listing; required fields are enforced and a name collision in scope is refused
behaviors.observable_outcome: 201 with {id, name, type:'codemod'}; the row appears in GET /api/codemods with type==='codemod'; 400 when name or script missing; 409 when an existing type='codemod' name collides in scope

behaviors.statement: The codemod listing returns only agent_skills discriminated as type='codemod', never plain skills
behaviors.observable_outcome: 200 array where every item has type==='codemod'

behaviors.statement: Fetching a saved codemod by id returns it, or 404 for an unknown id
behaviors.observable_outcome: 200 with the codemod when the id exists; 404 with error when it does not

permissions.notes: single-user local app; no RBAC. The only meaningful 'permission' is the repoPath write-confinement boundary, modelled as codemods.apply.confine-writes, not a role check.

error_states.statement: Invalid LLM-emitted transform code fails loudly at compile time as a ValidationError (new AsyncFunction wrap), not mid-apply
Apply target outside repoPath -> ValidationError 'Refusing to write outside the project' (400)
>100 TS files without overrideLimit -> ValidationError requesting confirmation
Saving a codemod whose name already exists as type='codemod' in scope -> 409

external_integrations: ts-morph (AST engine; LLM code calls its API directly)
agent_skills table + agent-skill.repository (saved-codemod persistence, overloaded via type discriminator)

unknowns.why_unknown: the E2E project is small; not exercised live, no test triggers the >100 path
