unknowns.why_unknown: the only preview test is a deliberate no-op and tolerates a 500 (AI unavailable in CI); transform correctness and diff fidelity are never asserted

unknowns.question: Is the saved-codemod re-run path (preview with a supplied script) reachable from the UI?
unknowns.why_unknown: panel saves but exposes no visible list/pick/re-run control; capability exists at the API only (domain doc Risks)

unknowns.question: Is the 500-line per-file diff cap (preview truncated, apply writes full content) observable/asserted anywhere?
unknowns.why_unknown: no test exercises a >500-line file; cosmetic/trust gap flagged in domain doc, unverified

capabilities.name: codemods

capabilities.business_purpose: The single sanctioned seam to git for a board that runs many AI agents in parallel against one real repository: give each agent an isolated worktree to commit in, inspect that work (diff incl. untracked), decide read-only whether it can land (conflict detection via merge-tree), and land it safely (plumbing merge / rebase / migration-renumber) without ever corrupting the human's checkout, losing committed work, or committing conflict markers. Every documented invariant is a real production incident encoded as a guard.

actors.name: server-services
actors.notes: workspace/review/merge services + deterministic monitor consume the facade via the server re-export shim

actors.name: mcp-agent
actors.notes: agents call git-backed MCP tools (get_workspace_diff, start_workspace) through the MCP re-export shim

actors.name: cli
actors.notes: pnpm cli reaches the same facade (from the main checkout)

actors.name: operator
actors.notes: single local user; triggers indirectly via UI buttons (New Workspace, Merge, diff viewer, Crime Scene / Hot Files)

entry_points.observable: whole high-level API: createWorktree/removeWorktree/mergeBranch/detectConflicts/getWorkingTreeDiff/rebaseOntoBase/prepareForReview/autoRenumberMigrations/branch+history helpers
the ONLY git spawn site; never-throw / throw / sync contracts, windowsHide, buffer/timeout/env/stdin
validate+register a repo (default-branch detect, git-root resolve), compute churn/hotspot metrics
derive a legal feature/ak-<n>-<slug> branch name from an issue
creates worktree+branch; returns diff; lands branch — all delegate to this module
returns the worktree diff vs base produced by getDiff/getWorkingTreeDiff

behaviors.statement: Creating a workspace provisions an isolated worktree checked out on its own branch cut from the requested base, with a guaranteed non-detached HEAD; an existing healthy worktree for the same branch is reused (same path returned), not duplicated
behaviors.observable_outcome: worktree directory exists with the base branch's files; createWorktree returns the path; a second call for the same branch returns the same path

behaviors.statement: A reused branch with NO unique commits beyond base is hard-refreshed onto the advanced base; a reused branch WITH its own commits is left untouched (never discarded)
preconditions: branch already exists from a prior, possibly stale, base
behaviors.observable_outcome: never-built branch tip == new base after createWorktree; branch with own work keeps its own tip

behaviors.statement: Removing a worktree breaks every junction (top-level and nested packages/*/node_modules) before deleting and never recurses into a link, refuses any path not strictly inside .worktrees/, and falls back to a manual recursive delete when `git worktree remove` fails — so the shared dependency store is never touched
preconditions: a worktree dir (possibly with junctions/leftover node_modules)
behaviors.observable_outcome: worktree dir gone; junction target dirs + their sentinel files survive; an unsafe path throws 'Refusing to recursively remove unsafe worktree path' and is left intact

behaviors.statement: A clean branch lands via a plumbing merge (merge-tree→commit-tree→update-ref): the target ref advances, and when the target is checked out the working tree is synced to the merge commit and left clean
preconditions: feature branch with commits ahead of base
behaviors.observable_outcome: result contains 'plumbing-merge'; base ref + checked-out working tree contain the feature file; status --porcelain empty; no uncommitted tracked changes

behaviors.statement: A conflicting merge is refused via three independent gates (stage-entry parse, merge-tree exit-1, marker scan of changed feature blobs excluding .md): it throws, never advances the target ref, never creates MERGE_HEAD, and never commits conflict markers into history or the working tree
preconditions: two branches with overlapping edits to a shared file (incl. drizzle _journal.json)
behaviors.observable_outcome: mergeBranch rejects /conflict/i; target ref unchanged; no MERGE_HEAD; working-tree + every historical blob free of <<<<<<< / ======= / >>>>>>>

behaviors.statement: Merging a branch already reachable from the target is idempotent — no new commit, ref/count unchanged — and it self-heals an interrupted plumbing merge (ref advanced but working tree never reset) by syncing the clean checked-out target forward
preconditions: feature branch already an ancestor of target (possibly with a desynced checkout)
behaviors.observable_outcome: result says 'already merged'; target ref + commit count unchanged across a second merge; an interrupted-merge checkout gets the missing files back

behaviors.statement: Merging into a checked-out target that has uncommitted tracked changes is refused up-front rather than reset --hard over the user's work
preconditions: target branch checked out in the main checkout with an uncommitted tracked edit
behaviors.observable_outcome: mergeBranch rejects /uncommitted tracked change/; the local edit survives byte-for-byte

behaviors.statement: The post-merge working-tree sync only ever moves forward: it resolves a safe sync target (never reset --hard to a SHA that isn't a descendant of HEAD, so a stale deferred sync can't delete files a concurrent merge added — #771) and self-heals tracked files deleted relative to HEAD after an interrupted hard sync (#692)
preconditions: deferred working-tree sync queued; possibly a later merge advanced HEAD first, or an interrupted reset left files deleted
behaviors.observable_outcome: a late applyDeferredWorkingTreeSync(stale-sha) deletes nothing; files added by the concurrent merge + the restored deleted files all present; status clean

behaviors.statement: A merge whose ONLY conflicts are pure tail-appends to shared hot files auto-resolves by concatenation (seeded from the conflicted merged tree, so the feature's clean files are NOT dropped) — but only behind the explicit autoResolveAppendConflicts opt-in; any overlapping EDIT still throws
preconditions: a wave of branches each appending distinct blocks to one shared file
behaviors.observable_outcome: result contains 'append-merge'; master holds every ticket's appended block + each branch's own feature file; without the flag the same conflict throws; an edit-conflict throws even with the flag

behaviors.statement: Conflict detection is read-only via merge-tree --write-tree: detectConflicts / detectConflictsByBranch / detectAppendOnlyResolvableConflicts report conflicting files (or append-resolvable hot files) without moving HEAD, touching the index/working tree, or creating MERGE_HEAD
behaviors.observable_outcome: hasConflicts + conflictingFiles correct; HEAD, status --porcelain, and file content identical before/after; no MERGE_HEAD

behaviors.statement: Diffing a branch/worktree surfaces its changes vs base including UNTRACKED files (hand-synthesized diff headers), and a direct workspace diffs via git diff HEAD; diff-stat ref semantics match the board (worktree→base, direct→HEAD)
preconditions: a worktree (or direct workspace) with committed + untracked changes
behaviors.observable_outcome: getWorkingTreeDiff output contains 'diff --git', 'new file mode', and the untracked file's added lines; getDiffShortstat called with the right (path, base) per workspace kind

behaviors.statement: Commits made from a detached HEAD in a worktree are recovered: syncBranchToHead force-updates the branch ref to the detached commit, and ensureOnBranch reattaches so subsequent merges aren't silent no-ops
preconditions: a worktree left on a detached HEAD with a new commit
behaviors.observable_outcome: branch ref == detached commit after syncBranchToHead (returns true); current branch == feature branch after ensureOnBranch; the commit's file content is on disk

behaviors.statement: Before merge, a feature branch's added drizzle migrations are auto-renumbered above the base's highest (all-or-nothing, multiple shifted without clobber) and _journal.json is rebuilt as base-prefix + renumbered entries, turning a guaranteed collision into a clean merge; no-op when no migrations were added; idempotent
preconditions: base advanced a migration (NNNN) while the feature added its own NNNN
behaviors.observable_outcome: renames reported (0001_b→0002_b, 0002_c→0003_c); journal tags ordered base-prefix-first; the previously-conflicting merge now succeeds; second renumber makes no commit; no-mig branch unchanged

behaviors.statement: Repo detection resolves the path to the git toplevel (subdirectory registration yields the same project), prefers default branch main-then-master (null if neither exists), reads the origin remote, and throws on a non-git directory
preconditions: a directory path (git or not), possibly a subdirectory of the repo
behaviors.observable_outcome: repoPath == git root; defaultBranch main>master or null; remoteUrl from origin; 'Not a git repository' thrown for non-git

behaviors.statement: Project git stats compute commit count, recent commits, source-tree LOC (prod vs test), 12-week history, and churn-ranked hotspots; the async twin dedupes concurrent cold computes into one shared in-flight promise and shares a 60s HEAD-keyed cache with the sync twin; a dormant repo falls back to a bounded full-history hotspot scan
preconditions: a repo with commits (possibly all older than the 12-week window)
behaviors.observable_outcome: commitCount/codeMetrics/history/hotspots populated and equal sync↔async; concurrent calls return the SAME object references; old-only repo still yields hotspots via fallback; fallback log args capped by --max-count

behaviors.statement: The git CLI is spawnable from exactly one adapter (git-exec.ts); an AST gate scans every package src/ and fails if any other module spawns git directly (via literal, const, variable, promisify alias, namespace, or dynamic import), keeping the 'single source of truth' true
behaviors.observable_outcome: the gate reports zero offenders outside the allowlisted adapter, and still detects the adapter itself (allowlist is live, not stale)

behaviors.statement: Branch names are derived deterministically from an issue: sanitizeBranchName lowercases, replaces spaces, collapses/trims hyphens, keeps slashes, truncates to 80; suggestBranchName yields feature/ak-<n>-<slug> (slug capped 40, issue number omitted when absent)
behaviors.observable_outcome: exact branch strings, e.g. feature/ak-3-fix-the-big-bug; 80-char / 40-char truncation enforced

behaviors.statement: Preparing a branch for review rebases onto the LOCAL base branch (not origin/*), first committing any leftover uncommitted worktree changes instead of bailing on a dirty tree, aborting a stale rebase, fetching best-effort, and on conflict collecting --diff-filter=U files then aborting to leave the tree clean
preconditions: a feature worktree, possibly dirty, possibly conflicting with an advanced local base
behaviors.observable_outcome: rebaseOntoBase succeeds on a dirty tree and leaves status clean; (untested) rebases onto local master ahead of origin and returns the conflicting-file list on conflict without leaving a half-rebase

permissions.notes: single-user local-first app — no RBAC; permission dimension is N/A for this capability

transitions.trigger: mergeBranch on conflict (refused, no state change)

error_states.statement: mergeBranch throws /conflict/i and leaves the target ref + working tree untouched (no MERGE_HEAD, no markers)
mergeBranch throws /uncommitted tracked change/ rather than reset over the user's edit
removeWorktree throws 'Refusing to recursively remove unsafe worktree path' for any path not inside .worktrees/
detectRepoInfo throws 'Not a git repository' for a non-git directory
(guarded) a detached-HEAD worktree would make commits dangle and merges silent no-ops; recovered by branch-attach
a stale on-disk .git/index.lock makes all git ops fail and is easily mistaken for a DB lock; operational recovery only, not code-guarded

external_integrations: filesystem (node:fs — untracked diff, worktree dirs/junctions, journal read/write)

unknowns.why_unknown: only rebaseOntoBase's dirty-tree leftover-commit path is exercised by an integration test; the local-vs-origin choice and conflict-list return are not directly asserted

unknowns.why_unknown: domain doc flags it as inferred-unverified; no test passes the default base on a master repo

unknowns.question: Are the untracked-diff synthesis edge cases (binary/unreadable file → header-only, trailing-newline off-by-one) correct?
unknowns.why_unknown: only the nominal text-untracked case is asserted; edge inputs not exercised

unknowns.question: Is the append-resolve concatenation ORDER (target-tail then feature-tail) ever wrong for an order-sensitive appended file?
unknowns.why_unknown: order is chosen as 'deterministic' not semantic; only order-insensitive smoke-test appends are tested

capabilities.name: git-integration

capabilities.business_purpose: The machine-facing front door of the board: an AI coding agent (Builder in a worktree, the Conductor monitor, a Smith analysis session) speaks Model Context Protocol over stdio and gets the same board operations a human gets in the UI/REST — file/move issues, spin up/merge/relaunch workspaces, query 'what are my agents doing', manage agent-skills, capture evidence — as named, typed tools, so the autonomous loop can manage its own work without a human or hand-rolled HTTP. The module owns no tables; its value is the policies it enforces at the agent boundary (active-project resolution, terminal-status merge-loss guard, workflow-edge legality, delegate-don't-reimplement for dangerous ops).

actors.notes: Builder/Conductor/Smith — Claude Code, Codex, Copilot; the only real caller. Speaks JSON-RPC over stdio.

actors.name: settings-operator
actors.notes: Indirectly shrinks the surface via the disabled_mcp_tools preference (Settings tool browser); no per-agent auth otherwise.

returns active project info + issue counts by status + active-workspace count

entry_points.observable: DB-direct issue CRUD + notifyBoard ping; bare-array output for list tools
fast structured pre-check then HTTP-delegate to REST :3001 for git/session ops
skill CRUD written to .claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md; name validation
HTTP front over the per-project warm Butler session
per-issue agent state projection from shared classifiers

behaviors.statement: get_context orients an agent: returns the active project, issue counts by status, and an active-workspace count
preconditions: an active project preference is set
behaviors.observable_outcome: JSON with project{id,name}, status counts, activeWorkspaces; the active project resolves from the activeProjectId pref (not the first-inserted project)

behaviors.statement: Every project-scoped tool resolves to the passed projectId, else the activeProjectId pref, else fails with an actionable 'No active project' fix-it message
behaviors.observable_outcome: with no projectId and no active pref → error text 'No active project'; with the pref set → operation lands in the active project, not the first inserted

behaviors.statement: create_issue inserts an issue with a per-project auto-incremented issue number and pings the board
behaviors.observable_outcome: returns the new issue (issueNumber increments per project); row persisted; notifyBoard(projectId,'mcp_create_issue') fired

behaviors.statement: create_issues_batch atomically seeds N issues plus dependency/coupling edges in one transaction, rolling back entirely on any validation failure and rejecting cycles/self/duplicate edges
behaviors.observable_outcome: consecutive issue numbers from current max; on validation failure (out-of-range index, self-dep, duplicate, cross-batch cycle) NOTHING persists

behaviors.statement: list_issues returns a project's issues as a bare JSON array, optionally filtered by status name
behaviors.observable_outcome: content[0].text parses to an Array (not {issues}); status filter narrows to matching issues

behaviors.statement: update_issue edits fields and walks status by name, reporting which fields changed and pinging the board
behaviors.observable_outcome: updated[] contains changed field keys (priority/statusId); DB reflects them; unknown status name → 'not found' error listing valid names

behaviors.statement: Moving an issue to a terminal status (Done/Cancelled) is blocked when it has an open, non-direct, unmerged workspace — the agent is told to merge_workspace first; direct/closed/no workspaces are allowed
behaviors.observable_outcome: blocked move returns code OPEN_WORKSPACE_NOT_MERGED; closed/merged/direct/no-workspace moves succeed (movedTo=Done)

behaviors.statement: A workflow-driven issue may only move along a legal outgoing edge of its current node; an illegal jump is refused with the valid next stages and a pointer to propose_transition
preconditions: issue is on a workflow graph (currentNodeId set) with outgoing transitions
behaviors.observable_outcome: illegal target → code WORKFLOW_TRANSITION_INVALID with the list of valid next stages; legal target proceeds

behaviors.statement: merge_workspace fast-fails missing/closed workspaces locally, then delegates the authoritative merge to the REST server (lock/backup/conflict-recovery) instead of re-implementing it
behaviors.observable_outcome: missing → WORKSPACE_NOT_FOUND, closed → WORKSPACE_CLOSED, no workingDir → WORKSPACE_WORKING_DIR_MISSING (no fetch); valid → POST /api/workspaces/:id/merge result passed through

behaviors.statement: launch_workspace / relaunch_workspace pre-validate workspace state then delegate the spawn to the REST server; relaunch refuses a non-idle workspace
behaviors.observable_outcome: relaunch on active ws → WORKSPACE_NOT_IDLE (no fetch); structured not-found/closed/no-dir errors; valid → REST /launch result

behaviors.statement: get_board_status answers 'what are my agents doing right now' — totals plus per-in-progress-issue workspace state and computed diff stats, excluding Done/Cancelled unless includeClosed, with no MCP-side cache
behaviors.observable_outcome: totals{totalIssues,inProgress,activeWorkspaces,runningSessions}; per-issue workspace.branch + diffStats; includeClosed toggles terminal issues; reads reflect DB immediately after a status change

behaviors.statement: delete_issue / delete_workspace remove the entity and every scoped child row (sessions, messages, diff comments, artifacts, comments, repos, transitions, retry decisions, tags, dependencies) through the shared cascade, leaving siblings intact
behaviors.observable_outcome: {id,deleted:true}; all child tables emptied for that entity; other issues/workspaces untouched; notifyBoard fired

behaviors.statement: attach_artifact records proof-of-work (text/link/image/webm) keyed to an issue or resolved from a workspaceId, surfacing it on the board
behaviors.observable_outcome: artifact row persisted with type/content/caption; workspaceId resolves to its issue; notifyBoard('mcp_attach_artifact')

behaviors.statement: update_dependencies_batch / contract_coupled_issues mutate the dependency graph idempotently, rejecting cycles and self-dependencies and refusing to contract components with open workspaces
behaviors.observable_outcome: existing edges skipped not failed; cycle/self rejected with no mutation; coupled contraction blocked when a member has an open workspace or the selection is partial

behaviors.statement: The butler_* tool family is a thin HTTP front over the per-project warm Butler session (interrupt/set-model/set-profile/state), surfacing server error bodies and a friendly message when the REST server is unreachable
preconditions: projectId; REST server reachable for success
behaviors.observable_outcome: POST/GET to /projects/:id/butler/* returns the server JSON; non-2xx body propagated; ECONNREFUSED → 'Failed to reach the butler'

behaviors.statement: The advertised MCP_TOOL_DEFINITIONS catalog and the runtime tools/list stay in exact name-parity in BOTH directions, and every runtime tool ships a non-empty description
behaviors.observable_outcome: advertised ⊆ registered (no phantom tool like the old mark_ready_for_merge) AND registered ⊆ advertised; mark_ready_for_merge present; no blank descriptions

behaviors.statement: create_agent_skill rejects names containing '/', '\' or '..' (they become filesystem paths) and enforces per-scope uniqueness before writing SKILL.md
behaviors.observable_outcome: path-traversal/illegal name → validation error, no skill written; duplicate name in scope → rejected; valid → skill persisted

behaviors.statement: A tool whose name is in the disabled_mcp_tools preference is never registered, so it does not appear in tools/list and cannot be called for that session
behaviors.observable_outcome: the disabled tool is absent from tools/list; calling it returns method-not-found

behaviors.statement: A status-change mutation (move_issue/update_issue) fires the project's outbound webhook best-effort, but only after the URL is validated loopback-only (the single egress boundary against a malicious pref)
behaviors.observable_outcome: valid loopback URL → POST issue-status payload; non-loopback/non-http(s) host → not fired; mutation succeeds regardless of webhook outcome

behaviors.statement: Unhandled async rejections are caught and logged to stderr so the stdio process stays connected; a stray write must never corrupt stdout (the JSON-RPC stream)
behaviors.observable_outcome: the server keeps answering subsequent requests; logs go to stderr only; stdout stays pure JSON-RPC

permissions.notes: No per-agent auth — any process reaching kanban.db over stdio has full board authority. The only authority knob is the global disabled_mcp_tools list (coarse, per-key). Intentional for a local-first single-user app; permission is near-trivial as a dimension.

capabilities.name: mcp-server

capabilities.business_purpose: The board's control plane: the machinery that drives kanban tickets toward merged code with no human in the loop. It decides PER PROJECT how work auto-starts (the Start Mode gate), relaunches/merges/nudges in-flight work up to a WIP target, pulls the next dependency-ready ticket on a merge, and refills a starved backlog — while guaranteeing two mechanisms never double-start the same ticket into conflicting worktrees.

actors.notes: out-of-process loop.sh spawned/killed by the server; sole driver in conductor mode

behaviors.statement: Start Mode is the single per-project kill-switch for auto-start: manual stops ALL auto-start paths, monitor enables in-process starts, conductor keeps in-process OFF; explicit start_mode_<id> fully supersedes the global auto_monitor toggle.
preconditions: a per-project start_mode_<id> pref or legacy flags exist
behaviors.observable_outcome: resolveStartPolicy returns {mode, autoStartUnblocked, postMergeCascade, backlogRefill, scheduledRuns, source}; manual ⇒ all four capabilities false even with every legacy opt-in flag ON

behaviors.statement: A project with no explicit start_mode derives one from legacy prefs (board_autodrive OR (auto_monitor AND nudge_auto_start) ⇒ monitor; else manual); conductor is NEVER derived, only set explicitly; an unknown mode value falls back to derivation.
behaviors.observable_outcome: resolveStartPolicy returns source='derived' with the back-compat mode; conductor never appears from derivation

behaviors.statement: A dependent is auto-started only when every blocker reached a terminal status AND its work actually LANDED on the base branch (mergedAt / direct commit), not merely 'Done'; a fan-in dependent waits for ALL upstreams to land.
behaviors.observable_outcome: no workspace is POSTed for a Done-but-unmerged blocker; a workspace IS created once mergedAt is set or a missing blocker row is treated as not-ready

behaviors.statement: WIP counts only active/reviewing/fixing workspaces (not blocked usage-limit launches or idle zero-output launch failures), so the auto-start ceiling reflects real running agents; starts are also capped at maxNewStartsPerCycle.
behaviors.observable_outcome: auto-start launches up to the WIP target counting only active-state workspaces; a stale-idle backlog over the target still permits a fresh start (#815); never more than maxNewStarts per cycle

behaviors.statement: Drive/epic META issues are never auto-started as builders (detected by Drive.metaIssueId or parent_of/child_of edge, enforced per-issue AND in the candidate SQL); feature/enhancement tickets are excluded globally UNLESS the project is auto-driven; no-auto-start-tagged issues are always skipped.
preconditions: candidate issues of various types/tags exist
behaviors.observable_outcome: metas and tagged issues produce no workspace; feature-typed Backlog issues auto-start only on an auto-driven project

behaviors.statement: Wave/auto-started builders for an auto-driven project are forwarded planMode:false so they start in EXECUTE mode, matching the New-Workspace default; non-auto-driven projects keep the priority default.
behaviors.observable_outcome: the POST /api/workspaces body carries planMode:false for auto-driven projects only

behaviors.statement: Automatic landing of approved/idle workspaces requires auto_merge='true' AND merge strategy='monitor', with a per-project auto_merge_disabled_<id> override; manual POST /merge is never affected. Merges are capped per cycle with fix-and-merge fallback on conflict.
preconditions: idle workspace with readyForMerge OR reviewing+stopped
behaviors.observable_outcome: merge fires only when enabled and the project isn't disabled; falls back to fix-and-merge on conflict; no more than the merge cap per cycle

behaviors.statement: Un-ready In-Review/stopped-review work is auto-merged only after the pre-merge verify+smoke gate passes; a project with a verify_script or web stack profile is 'gated' and a failed gate WITHHOLDS the merge (leaves In Review).
behaviors.observable_outcome: projectHasMergeGate true ⇒ merge runs the gate at the merge site and does not land on failure (#821)

behaviors.statement: Stuck-builder recovery fires only for a builder session 0 commits ahead AND (non-trivial uncommitted diff OR repeated-failed-command retry loop): it commits leftover work and routes to review instead of killing progress; a workspace with >=10 sessions (>=5 In Review) is force-closed as stuck.
preconditions: active+running builder past the stuck timeout
behaviors.observable_outcome: leftover work committed + review launched; runaway relaunch/review loops force-closed

behaviors.statement: On a blocker merge, the post-merge dependency cascade (a THIRD auto-start path) selects exactly ONE Todo/Backlog issue whose deps all LANDED, respects the WIP cap, skips no-auto-start + dependency-cycle members, and records an audit comment — BUT is gated by the project's Start Mode: with manual it is a clean no-op (not even an audit comment) even when dependency_auto_chain=true.
behaviors.observable_outcome: one dependent workspace starts (or a skip is audited) when monitor mode; nothing happens at all under manual

behaviors.statement: The dependency-wave planner partitions candidates into ready / blocked / cyclicInvalid, starts only the next ready wave that FITS under the WIP target, reports per-dependent which upstreams are still un-landed, and never launches cycle members.
preconditions: a dependency graph of open issues
behaviors.observable_outcome: wave start launches a slice up to wip.available; blocked dependents list their specific blockers; cyclic candidates are invalid

behaviors.statement: Backlog refill generates synthetic tickets only when unstarted-Todo < backlog floor AND WIP < target AND a cooldown (default 120min) elapsed AND backlog_empty_strategy='generate_tickets'; the host issue is born In Progress (can't re-trigger itself) and the cooldown is stamped only on a successful launch.
behaviors.observable_outcome: a generation-skill workspace launches and stamps cooldown; nothing happens when backlog has unstarted issues, WIP is full, cooldown active, or strategy is skip/unset; orphan host deleted on launch failure

behaviors.statement: An opt-in pre-fan-out step contracts coupled_with tickets into one so they never start as conflicting parallel workspaces; contract mode is off by default and resolved per project (apply/suggest/true ⇒ on).
behaviors.observable_outcome: coupled components merged before fan-out; batch dependency validation rejects cycles/self/duplicate/out-of-range edges

behaviors.statement: The one-switch Drive flips a COHERENT pref set atomically (autodrive on, kill-switch off, review+merge on, planMode off, verify gate ensured) and on OFF restores triage and re-arms the kill-switch; provider/profile is intentionally NOT owned by Drive, and an existing user verify-script override is preserved.
behaviors.observable_outcome: PUT /drive {enabled} writes every owned pref together; PUT without boolean enabled is rejected; provider survives a triage⇄drive flip

behaviors.statement: Drive preflight reports the machine-checkable prerequisites and with autoRepair flips Drive on ONLY when EVERY blocker is one-switch-fixable; hard blockers (null defaultBranch, missing status set, exhausted/mock Claude profile) are reported honestly and never silently worked around.
preconditions: a project in any readiness state
behaviors.observable_outcome: a freshly-registered project lists what's missing; autoRepair flips on and reports ready when repairable; hard blockers keep it blocked and surface them (WIP=1 warns but doesn't block)

behaviors.statement: POST /conductor start spawns loop.sh detached (survives hot-reload), records the OS PID, clears the stop-marker, and is a NO-OP if one is already alive; stop tree-kills by PID plus a PowerShell backstop reaping every loop.sh and drops a stop-marker so read-only status reports 'stopped' immediately.
preconditions: a project with a Conductor objective
behaviors.observable_outcome: starting twice does not spawn a second loop; stopping makes the orchestrator status flip to not-alive

behaviors.statement: Read-only Conductor liveness is computed from disk: alive when loop.log mtime is fresh (<11min) and no newer stop-marker exists; a restart's fresh log supersedes a stale marker; a stale log reads not-alive regardless of marker.
preconditions: a Conductor state directory on disk
behaviors.observable_outcome: GET /orchestrator reports alive/not-alive consistent with log/marker mtimes

behaviors.statement: A cycle never overlaps itself (re-entrancy guard): a trigger arriving mid-cycle schedules exactly one more pass at the end; a maintenance window suppresses all disruptive actions without disabling the monitor.
preconditions: concurrent triggers OR an active maintenance window
behaviors.observable_outcome: two concurrent triggers never both POST a workspace for the same issue; during maintenance no relaunch/merge/start fires

behaviors.statement: The Monitor Butler runs a fresh, stateless Claude Agent SDK session on a schedule (10-min hard timeout) that snapshots the board, reads strategy from objective.md, acts through MCP tools, and logs every tool call + start/end to board_health_events; a crashed cycle never poisons the next, and it drives only ONE active project per cycle.
behaviors.observable_outcome: board_health_events rows appear per cycle; only the active project is acted on

set start_mode=manual or Drive OFF (true kill-switch)

generation workspace launch fails ⇒ orphan host issue deleted, cooldown NOT stamped
preflight reports a non-auto-repairable blocker (null defaultBranch / no statuses / exhausted/mock profile); Drive stays off
PUT /drive without a boolean enabled is rejected

capabilities.name: monitor-orchestration

capabilities.business_purpose: The single physical truth of the product: one SQLite file (kanban.db) holding every project, issue, workspace, session, message and preference. As a Published Language the Drizzle schema is the shared data vocabulary every package imports; as a runtime kernel it provides the connection, the busy-retry policy under heavy concurrent agent writes, migration management, FK-integrity (cascade/drift/repair), application-level cascade deletion, and a verified rotating-backup recovery subsystem. Its correctness guarantees are what keep the board from silently corrupting when many parallel worktree agents stream output into one file.

actors.name: operator
actors.notes: single local user; runs db:repair / db:restore CLI

actors.name: services
actors.notes: every server/MCP/CLI module reads/writes through db/writeDb handles and the schema

actors.name: parallel-agents
actors.notes: many worktree agent subprocesses streaming session_messages concurrently into one DB — the contention source

actors.name: startup-tasks
actors.notes: boot-time FK-enable assertion + FK-action alignment + pre-migration backup

actors.name: backup-scheduler
actors.notes: periodic verified snapshots + rotation

entry_points.observable: Published-Language: row types every package shares; a new column is a fact the whole product can reason about
typed read/write queries against kanban.db; separate WAL reader/writer connections
wraps a DB op or transaction in bounded SQLITE_BUSY retry
atomically remove an entity and its whole dependent subtree, asserting nothing remains
detects + rebuilds FK-action drift on a legacy live DB without data loss
lists/verifies/restores a backup snapshot into kanban.db
resolves where kanban.db lives; worktree falls through to ~/.agentic-kanban
journaled migration list in apply order; drives in-memory test DB

behaviors.statement: Applying every journaled migration to an empty DB succeeds and reproduces the core schema (tables exist)
preconditions: journal lists migrations in apply order
behaviors.observable_outcome: no error thrown; expected tables (e.g. workspaces) present after apply

behaviors.statement: A freshly-migrated DB and the Drizzle schema agree on the table set and per-table column set (no schema column without a migration, and the reverse)
preconditions: all migrations applied to in-memory DB
behaviors.observable_outcome: missingTables/extraTables empty; no per-table columnDrift

behaviors.statement: The ON DELETE/ON UPDATE actions the migrations produce equal the actions the Drizzle schema declares (no schema-says-cascade / DB-on-NO-ACTION drift)
behaviors.observable_outcome: diffForeignKeyActions returns no mismatches

behaviors.statement: The migration journal is structurally sound: no duplicate NNNN numbers, no orphan .sql un-journaled files, every journal entry has a file, unique tags/idx
behaviors.observable_outcome: no duplicate numbers, no orphans, journal/disk are bijective, tags+idx unique

behaviors.statement: A locked DB is treated as transient: SQLITE_BUSY/EBUSY/'database is locked' is retried up to 3 attempts (50ms then 100ms) and then re-throws; a non-busy error is not retried
preconditions: operation rejects with a busy-class error (by code or message substring)
behaviors.observable_outcome: succeeds on a later attempt and returns the result; after 3 busy attempts re-throws the original; non-busy error throws after exactly 1 attempt with no retry log

behaviors.statement: Deleting an issue removes its entire dependent subtree (workspaces, sessions, messages, diff-comments, artifacts, comments, time-entries, showdowns, dependencies both directions, tags) atomically and leaves no orphan or FK violation
preconditions: issue with seeded children across every issue/workspace-referencing table
behaviors.observable_outcome: issue + all children gone; sibling issue untouched; PRAGMA foreign_key_check returns no rows

behaviors.statement: Deleting a workspace removes every table that references the workspace or its sessions, atomically, while leaving the parent issue intact
behaviors.observable_outcome: workspace + sessions gone; parent issue remains; no FK violations

behaviors.statement: db:repair rebuilds a legacy table whose FK actions drifted (e.g. issue_dependencies on NO ACTION) to the schema's cascade, preserving all rows and user indexes, making the cascade actually fire, and is idempotent
preconditions: a live DB with a drifted FK action and existing rows
behaviors.observable_outcome: drift detected; table rebuilt; rows + unique index survive; deleting parent now cascades; a second run finds no drift

behaviors.statement: At startup FK enforcement is asserted per connection (throws LOUD if PRAGMA foreign_keys is OFF) and FK-action drift on the live DB is repaired before serving
preconditions: possibly a legacy drifted live DB
behaviors.observable_outcome: passes when FK=ON; throws a connection-named error when OFF; legacy drift is rebuilt so the schema's cascades fire; a matched DB is a silent no-op

behaviors.statement: Issue numbers are unique per project (not global): a unique index on (project_id, issue_number) lets two projects each have a #1, and next-number allocation is MAX+1 scoped per project
behaviors.observable_outcome: nextIssueNumber returns max+1 within a project and is unaffected by other projects; a duplicate (project_id, issue_number) insert is rejected by the DB unique index

behaviors.statement: createBackup writes a WAL-consistent, committed-only single-file snapshot (VACUUM INTO) and verifies it before accepting: integrity_check ok and row counts match the live source
behaviors.observable_outcome: returns a verified backup whose project/issue counts equal the source; an in-flight uncommitted write is excluded from the snapshot

behaviors.statement: A backup is refused unless trustworthy: a corrupt/truncated file fails integrity_check, and an empty-when-live-is-not snapshot (0 projects or 0 issues while live has rows) is rejected rather than rotated in; a failed verify never promotes and preserves the previous good backup
preconditions: candidate backup is corrupt or empty
behaviors.observable_outcome: verifyBackup throws on corrupt / 0-projects / 0-issues; a forced verify failure leaves the prior good backup intact and no .tmp scratch behind

behaviors.statement: Backups rotate safely: keep exactly KEEP_LAST(5) newest, never prune to zero, enforce a hard total-size cap keeping at least one, and reap interrupted-write scratch (.tmp/.promote + journal sidecars) without harming legitimate final-backup sidecars
preconditions: backup dir with >KEEP_LAST files and/or orphan scratch
behaviors.observable_outcome: 5 newest survive; size-cap trims oldest but never to zero; scratch removed, good backups + their sidecars kept

behaviors.statement: A wipe is recoverable: backup -> wipe -> verify+restore reproduces the original row counts (local-only durability guarantee)
preconditions: a verified backup of seeded data
behaviors.observable_outcome: restored DB project/issue counts equal the pre-wipe live DB

behaviors.statement: The DB file is resolved by existence (env override -> local checkout -> ~/.agentic-kanban), so a worktree dev-server transparently runs against a separate home-dir DB rather than the main board
preconditions: no DB_URL/AGENTIC_KANBAN_DIR override; checkout may or may not have a local kanban.db
behaviors.observable_outcome: getDbUrl returns the checkout DB when present, else the home-dir DB; a worktree (no checked-out db) gets a different database/project-IDs

permissions.notes: single-user local app — no RBAC; permission dimension is trivial (one actor)

transitions.trigger: db:repair / startup FK alignment rebuilds tables

error_states.statement: PRAGMA foreign_keys OFF on a connection throws a LOUD connection-named error at startup
After 3 busy attempts the original lock error is re-thrown ('giving up after 3 attempts')
If a child row survives a cascade walk, assert*CascadeComplete throws (deletion incompleteness is a hard error)
A corrupt or empty-when-live-is-not backup is rejected by verifyBackup and never promoted

capabilities.name: persistence-schema

capabilities.business_purpose: The board's settings substrate: a flat (key,value) preference store plus the layered resolution that turns those strings into effective board behaviour — which agent provider/profile/model launches a workspace, whether and how aggressively a project auto-starts work, how the monitor balances feature-vs-bugfix, and what board config is portable. Its reason to exist is that one logical setting is rarely one row, so it is the layered resolver that collapses scattered inputs (Strategy Bullseye, global prefs, per-workspace override, legacy flags) into one effective answer every consumer agrees on. Also the system's most drift-prone surface, so several invariants exist purely to fail loudly instead of silently dropping/diverging.

preconditions: key is in SETTINGS_KEYS or matches a recognized dynamic prefix

preconditions: write touches provider or *_profile and would diverge
behaviors.observable_outcome: 422 {ok:false, divergence:{bullseyeProvider, settingsProvider,...}}; provider/profile prefs unchanged in DB

behaviors.statement: The divergence guard fires ONLY when the write touches a provider/profile key, and is a no-op when the active project has no Bullseye
preconditions: a pre-existing untouched drift exists, OR no Bullseye configured
behaviors.observable_outcome: an unrelated toggle save returns 200 despite drift; a provider write with no Bullseye returns 200 and persists

behaviors.statement: Provider/profile is resolved by a fixed precedence: explicit per-workspace override > legacy claudeProfile > Strategy Bullseye selection > global settings pref
preconditions: a workspace launch resolves its provider config from prefs
behaviors.observable_outcome: resolved {provider, profileName} follows precedence; an unknown provider string narrows to claude

behaviors.statement: Model is provider-scoped only: the retired global default_model is never read, and a model not belonging to the resolved provider is dropped to the provider default
preconditions: prefs may carry a stale global default_model and/or provider-scoped default_model_<provider>
behaviors.observable_outcome: only default_model_<provider> (or requestedModel) drives the model; global default_model has no effect; mismatch → undefined (provider default)

behaviors.statement: Start Mode is the single auto-start kill-switch: manual stops ALL auto-start, conductor forces in-process auto-start off, monitor enables it; an unset mode is derived from legacy flags (conductor never derived)
behaviors.observable_outcome: StartPolicy exposes autoStartUnblocked/postMergeCascade/backlogRefill/scheduledRuns booleans + source; manual → all false, conductor → all in-process false (scheduledRuns true)

behaviors.statement: A saved Strategy Bullseye derives the monitor's effective dials (active-agents target, backlog floor, max-new-starts, refill focus); bugfix-heavy weights yield bugfix-only refill; malformed/absent Bullseye falls back to legacy nudge prefs
preconditions: board_strategy_<id> JSON present (valid, malformed, or absent)
behaviors.observable_outcome: resolveMonitorTunables returns {tunables, source:'strategy'|'prefs'}; malformed JSON silently falls through to legacy with source='prefs'

behaviors.statement: Bullseye provider routing picks fill > throttle > fallback-only, skipping any policy blocked by live quota, and degrades gracefully (static order) when quota telemetry is missing
behaviors.observable_outcome: selectProviderFromStrategy returns the highest-priority non-quota-blocked policy; null when only fallback-only and allowFallback false; quota=null → ignores quota

behaviors.statement: A per-harness behavior knob resolves scoped (harness.<h>.<key>) > legacy flat key > per-harness default, treating empty scoped values as unset
preconditions: harness-scoped and/or legacy flat pref present
behaviors.observable_outcome: getHarnessBoolSetting returns the scoped value when set, else legacy, else the harness default

behaviors.statement: The Claude-specific --dangerously-skip-permissions flag is appended only for the Claude provider; other providers' args are left untouched
behaviors.observable_outcome: Claude agentArgs gain the flag; Copilot/Codex/Pi do not

behaviors.statement: The active-project preference can be read and set (stored as-is, no validation against the project list) and scopes the divergence guard
behaviors.observable_outcome: PUT echoes and persists projectId (incl. null/fake ids); GET returns it as both projectId and value

behaviors.statement: Board config export bundles statuses + Bullseye + the 3 safe workflow prefs and omits secrets/profiles; import validates shape, supports dryRun preview, drops disallowed keys non-fatally, and round-trips onto a fresh project
behaviors.observable_outcome: export JSON excludes provider/claude_profile; import 400 on bad shape, dryRun returns statusChanges without applying, evil keys ignored, round-trip reproduces statuses+strategy

behaviors.statement: The provider-divergence read endpoint reports whether global provider/profile prefs drift from a project's Bullseye (hasBullseye=false when none configured or projectId omitted)
behaviors.observable_outcome: GET returns {hasBullseye, bullseye*, settings*, diverged}; diverged=true on provider or profile mismatch, false when they agree or no Bullseye

behaviors.statement: Per-project/freeform dynamic keys are recognized by prefix (project-scoped requires a hex/UUID suffix; freeform requires any non-empty suffix), so unknowable project ids are accepted while garbage is rejected
behaviors.observable_outcome: valid project-scoped keys persist and read back; non-hex/empty suffixes are rejected; board_strategy_<id> is handled separately

behaviors.statement: Live quota telemetry is surfaced from the external tampermonkey-direct service and degrades to 503 (with empty providers) when that source is unavailable
behaviors.observable_outcome: GET returns quota data (200) or {error, providers:[]} with 503 when the external endpoint is down

permissions.notes: single-user local app — no RBAC; permission dimension is N/A

transitions.trigger: Drive on / start_mode=monitor or derived from legacy flags

error_states.statement: PUT settings with an un-whitelisted key returns 422 + droppedKeys (valid keys still applied)
PUT settings with a diverging provider/profile returns 422 + divergence, nothing persisted
config import with an invalid shape returns 400
quota-usage returns 503 when the external telemetry source is down
INFERRED hazard: the write-time divergence guard fails OPEN on malformed Bullseye JSON (resolveProviderDivergence catches parse error → diverged:false), silently allowing the write it should block

external_integrations: git CLI (objective.md auto-commit on Bullseye save)

unknowns.why_unknown: the kill-switch claim is a monitor-orchestration concern; only the resolver's boolean outputs are observable here, and the manual/conductor branches are not directly asserted by any test

capabilities.name: preferences-config

capabilities.business_purpose: Onboard any git repo as a board project and reverse-engineer enough of its tech stack to drive it hands-off: create the project row + canonical statuses + non-null default branch + default skill, then derive every per-stack script the agent harness needs (install/setup, the merge-gate verify command, a boot-and-respond smoke check, edit-time feedback rules, a runnable green test scaffold, and clean-build/.gitignore hardening). This is the foundation that lets the board drive Node, Rust, Go, Python and JVM (Gradle/Kotlin/KMP/Spring/Ktor) repos, not just its own monorepo.

actors.name: operator
actors.notes: single local user registering a repo via UI 'Add Project' modal, CLI `pnpm cli -- register`, or MCP

actors.name: startup
actors.notes: boot-time deduplicateProjects() runs on every server start

actors.name: monitor
actors.notes: consumes the derived setup_script/verify_script downstream; only auto-starts a fully-driveable project

actors.name: review-merge
actors.notes: pre-merge gate runs the derived verify_script + smoke + opt-in cold-clone check

entry_points.observable: creates project (201 with {id,name,repoPath}); idempotent on same git root; fires best-effort profile/script derivation

entry_points.ref: Add Project modal (Import existing / Create new tabs)
entry_points.observable: register path or create+scaffold a new repo; errors render inline, modal stays open
registers a repo as a project
onboard or backfill driveable state + profile/scripts
marker-file → StackProfile descriptor
merges duplicate registrations sharing one git root at boot
clean-clone install+build pass/fail result

behaviors.statement: Registering a git repo creates a driveable project: a project row, canonical statuses, a default branch and default skill, returned synchronously as 201
preconditions: path is a valid git repo
behaviors.observable_outcome: POST /api/projects returns 201 with {id, name, repoPath}; project appears in GET /api/projects

behaviors.statement: Re-registering the same repo (or a subdirectory resolving to the same git root) returns the existing project instead of creating a duplicate
preconditions: a project already registered for that git root
behaviors.observable_outcome: result.created === false and the same project id is returned; no second row; issues/skills stay on one project

behaviors.statement: Registering with no repoPath (or a non-git path) is rejected with a 4xx and an explanatory error, leaving no project created
behaviors.observable_outcome: POST /api/projects returns 400 with error mentioning repoPath; duplicate path returns 409

behaviors.statement: A newly registered project never gets a null defaultBranch — it falls back to the repo's actually checked-out branch so the project is not silently undriveable (#772)
preconditions: repo's default branch is not named main/master, or detection returned null
behaviors.observable_outcome: project.defaultBranch is non-null when a branch is checked out; POST /api/workspaces does not 400 'No default branch configured'

behaviors.statement: Every new project is seeded the canonical 7-status set (incl. Backlog at -1) so issue batch-create and Backlog-pull auto-start work
behaviors.observable_outcome: GET /api/projects/:id/statuses lists Todo/In Progress/In Review/Done/Cancelled (+Backlog); POST /api/issues/batch does not 400 'No statuses found'

behaviors.statement: On startup, duplicate registrations resolving to one git root are merged into a single survivor — issues/skills/repos/scheduled-runs and the active-project pointer are moved, statuses remapped by name, duplicates deleted
preconditions: two project rows share one resolved git root (legacy subdir registration)
behaviors.observable_outcome: after boot only one project remains for that root; its issues/skills are intact; activeProjectId points at the survivor

behaviors.statement: repairProjectRegistration idempotently backfills driveable state onto an old/partial project — seeds statuses if none, sets defaultBranch if null, and populates profile/verify/setup scripts if unset — never clobbering existing values
preconditions: a project registered before profiles/scripts existed, or with missing statuses/branch
behaviors.observable_outcome: returns {seededStatuses, setDefaultBranch, populatedStackProfile, populatedVerifyScript, populatedSetupScript}; re-running is a no-op once filled

behaviors.statement: Marker files are reverse-engineered into one durable StackProfile across many stacks (node/pnpm/npm/yarn, rust/cargo, go, python/pip/poetry), inferring package manager, monorepo-ness, build/test/lint/dev commands, web-ness and dev port
behaviors.observable_outcome: detectStackProfile returns a typed profile with the correct stack/commands; empty repo yields a valid sparse profile (stack null, source 'detected')

behaviors.statement: The JVM/Gradle branch picks platform-correct, project-shape-correct commands: Kotlin vs Java compile task, KMP allTests vs test, Spring bootRun vs application run vs Ktor, .\gradlew.bat vs ./gradlew, dev-port scan, web-ness
behaviors.observable_outcome: profile reflects e.g. KMP allTests + no compileJava/dev; Ktor isWeb + run + port 8080/literal; Spring bootRun + isWeb; Java compileJava typecheck

behaviors.statement: The keystone merge gate is derived as `testCommand && buildCommand` (test first), persisted to verify_script_<projectId>, no-op on nothing-derivable, never clobbering a user/AI override
behaviors.observable_outcome: verify_script pref = 'pnpm test && pnpm build' (or test-only, or marker fallback); empty profile writes nothing; existing override survives

behaviors.statement: The monorepo-aware install/setup command is derived (pnpm install -r, gradle assemble, cargo fetch, …), persisted to projects.setup_script, no-op when empty and never clobbering an existing value
preconditions: a profile with installCommand or detectable markers
behaviors.observable_outcome: projects.setup_script = monorepo-aware install; single-package gets non-recursive install; empty detection leaves it null; existing value preserved

behaviors.statement: A boot-and-respond smoke check is built only for a bootable web/service project with a resolvable health URL — HTTP 200 plus HTML-shell needles for browser UIs, 200-only for headless APIs, null (skip) for CLI/library/no-URL
behaviors.observable_outcome: buildSmokeCheck returns {devCommand, healthUrl, expectBodyContains} for web UIs; [] needles for JSON APIs; null for non-web/no-URL

behaviors.statement: Edit-time feedback rules are generated cheapest-signal-first and scoped to the stack's source globs, with the slow-JVM family downgraded (no per-edit test rule, non-blocking typecheck)
behaviors.observable_outcome: .claude/smart-hooks-rules.json holds Typecheck/Quick-tests rules with stack-correct filePatterns; gradle gets no blocking per-edit test rule; no usable command → no rules

behaviors.statement: A trivially-passing runnable test is generated in the project's REAL test dir + runner syntax (vitest/pytest/cargo/go/JUnit/kotlin.test), and is never written into a dir that already has tests
behaviors.observable_outcome: writeTestScaffold writes one green test at the right path/extension; unknown stack writes nothing; existing tests are not clobbered/duplicated

behaviors.statement: New-project scaffold keeps main clean and clean-clones buildable: per-stack build-artifact + agent .gitignore (idempotent, no-clobber) and per-package-manager clean-build hardening (pnpm onlyBuiltDependencies, bun trustedDependencies, packageManager pin) so a fresh clone builds with no approval prompts
behaviors.observable_outcome: .gitignore gains the stack's artifact lines + agent lines once; package.json/pnpm config approves native postinstall + pins the manager; non-Node stacks are a clean no-op

behaviors.statement: The opt-in cold-clone build gate clones the committed branch into a fresh temp dir and runs install then build, failing the review on breakage that only appears on a truly clean clone, and always cleaning up the temp dir
preconditions: cold_clone_check_<projectId> enabled; a derivable build command
behaviors.observable_outcome: result.ok=false with reason build-failed/install-failed/clone-failed and failedCommand on breakage; ok=true 'no-build-command' when nothing derivable; temp clone always cleaned

behaviors.statement: When the rule-detected profile is sparse (no stack, or no test AND no build command) an LLM gap-fill is invoked and merged in ONLY where rules left null — rule facts win — and the whole derivation is fire-and-forget so it never slows or fails registration
behaviors.observable_outcome: persisted profile gains LLM-supplied commands only in null slots, source flips to 'llm'; registration returns immediately regardless of LLM outcome

permissions.notes: single-user local app — no RBAC; permission dimension is N/A (one actor)

error_states.statement: Registering a non-git path returns 4xx with 'not a git repository'
Re-registering an already-registered path returns 409 'already registered' (UI surfaces it inline)
cold-clone gate returns ok=false reason clone-failed/install-failed/build-failed
A hallucinated/hostile testCommand from a sparse-profile LLM gap-fill becomes the shell-executed verify gate with no allow-list — exploitation needs both a sparse-enough profile and a hostile command

external_integrations: claude-cli LLM (sparse-profile gap-fill + legacy generate* prompts)
filesystem (scaffold + smart-hooks + test-scaffold writes into the repo)

capabilities.name: project-registration

capabilities.business_purpose: The delivery boundary of the board: turns an AI builder's throwaway worktree branch into code on the project's default branch — AI code-review approval gate, gated auto-merge, conflict/fix-and-merge recovery, and a fleet of reconcilers that keep DB status honest with git reality. Exists to protect two invariants: 'a ticket is Done iff its code is reachable from the default branch' and 'nothing merges that wasn't approved (or explicitly allowed to skip approval)'.

actors.name: operator
actors.notes: single local user — manual /review, /merge, /fix-and-merge via UI or REST

actors.name: auto-merge-orchestrator
actors.notes: scheduled 30s batch engine, ON by default (merge_queue strategy)

actors.name: in-process-monitor
actors.notes: alternate auto-merge engine (merge_strategy=monitor); also runs the verify/smoke gate

actors.name: reviewer-agent
actors.notes: LLM that reads the diff and signals via mark_ready_for_merge; never self-merges

actors.name: fix-agent
actors.notes: relaunched in-worktree to resolve conflicts / clean tree for fix-and-merge

actors.name: reconcilers
actors.notes: interval+boot honesty restorers (done-unmerged, stranded-review, ancestor-branch, completion-state, zombie-fix)

entry_points.observable: launches an AI review session on an idle workspace; 200 + sessionId, or 409 when not idle
sets readyForMerge=true (the reviewer's approval signal); 404 unknown id
merges approved branch into base; 200 merged/reconciled, or 409 not_approved/conflict/dirty_main/already_merged
relaunches an agent in the worktree to fix a failed merge; workspace status → fixing
agentic conflict resolution / rebase base in
detect/close already-landed branch without a new merge commit
batch-merges approved/reviewed workspaces via the queue
committed-exit → issue to In Review → auto-review handshake
restore status↔git honesty (done-unmerged scan, stranded-review, ancestor-branch, completion-state, zombie-fix)

behaviors.statement: Launching a review starts an LLM review session on the builder's OWN provider/profile for an idle workspace; a non-idle workspace is refused
behaviors.observable_outcome: 200 with sessionId, workspace transitions reviewing; 409 ReviewError when not idle; review uses workspace provider/profile not the global default

behaviors.statement: A builder that exits having committed work moves its issue to In Review and (when auto_review on) launches a review session; the transition is idempotent
behaviors.observable_outcome: issue=In Review, board/workspace/workflow-node agree; repeated exit calls do not duplicate the review session or advance past In Review

behaviors.statement: A review that exits on a 0-commit branch must NOT approve it: readyForMerge stays false, workspace stays idle/In Review, no ready broadcast (#629)
preconditions: branch has no committed changes (re-verified via hasCommittedChanges)
behaviors.observable_outcome: readyForMerge=false; workspace idle, issue In Review; workspace_ready_for_merge NOT broadcast

behaviors.statement: The reviewer signals merge-readiness via mark_ready_for_merge (readyForMerge=true) and is contractually forbidden from merging itself, so the system's gates always run (#822)
preconditions: review session active on a workspace
behaviors.observable_outcome: readyForMerge flips true and is reflected in workspace GET + board summary; review prompt instructs verify+approve but NOT self-merge

behaviors.statement: Merging an approved workspace lands its branch on the base branch (advancing master), closes the workspace, and moves the issue to Done; concurrent requests to the same workspace are deduplicated
behaviors.observable_outcome: 200 {merged:true, mergeCommitSha, baseHeadShaBefore/After}; base branch advances; workspace closed; issue Done; concurrent /merge calls share one promise

behaviors.statement: Merging a non-direct workspace whose readyForMerge=false is refused with 409 not_approved — unless auto_merge_in_review bypasses the gate (and the project isn't gated)
behaviors.observable_outcome: 409 CONFLICT mergeReason=not_approved, no git merge; auto_merge_in_review=true proceeds past the gate; isDirect workspaces skip the gate unconditionally

behaviors.statement: A merge is blocked when the main checkout has any uncommitted tracked change — never merge on top of someone's WIP
preconditions: main checkout has uncommitted tracked changes
behaviors.observable_outcome: 409 CONFLICT mergeReason=dirty_main, mergeBranch never called; proceeds once the checkout is clean; checked even when workingDir is missing

behaviors.statement: A conflicting merge is refused with 409 conflict (listing conflict files), clears the stale readyForMerge flag, leaves the issue not-Done, and the server stays healthy
preconditions: branch conflicts with base (read-only merge-tree detection or behind-base rebase)
behaviors.observable_outcome: 409 CONFLICT with conflictFiles + behindCount; readyForMerge cleared; workspace not closed, issue not Done; no conflict markers spilled; server health endpoint responsive

behaviors.statement: When a branch tip is already an ancestor of base (with >=1 unique commit), /merge is a no-op reconcile: 200 {merged:false, reconciled:true}, NO new merge commit, issue→Done, stale review session closed; honored idempotently via mergedAt on dropped-response retries
preconditions: branch tip is ancestor of base
>=1 unique commit OR mergedAt already set
behaviors.observable_outcome: 200 merged:false reconciled:true with baseBranch+baseHeadSha; mergeBranch NOT called; issue Done; retry after dropped response converges without re-merging

behaviors.statement: A branch with 0 unique commits (branchSha===baseSha) is NOT reconciled-as-Done — it is kept In Review (reconciled:false / clean-ancestor) as a false-positive guard against silent-merge-loss
behaviors.observable_outcome: workspace stays open, issue stays In Review, reconciled:false; no Done transition, no merge commit

behaviors.statement: A failed/conflicting merge can be recovered by relaunching an agent in the worktree (status → fixing); on resolver exit the merge is re-attempted, the workspace stays retryable if the branch didn't land, or closes if it did; stale zero-output fix sessions are force-recovered
preconditions: caller chose recovery (POST /fix-and-merge) or resolver session exits
behaviors.observable_outcome: workspace status fixing; resolver exit without landing → workspace kept open+idle (retryable); resolver exit having landed → workspace closed, issue Done; zero-output zombie fix session force-stopped before retry

behaviors.statement: The scheduled auto-merge engine selects idle reviewed workspaces (incl. ready work stranded in Done), excludes direct/closed/in-progress/user-parked/auto_merge_disabled projects, and only runs when merge_strategy=merge_queue; In-Review idle work is included only when auto_merge_in_review is on
behaviors.observable_outcome: findCompletedWorkspaceIds returns exactly the eligible set; engine no-ops when strategy != merge_queue; zombie reconciler session reaped so next tick can relaunch

behaviors.statement: Batch execution lands clean siblings in least-overlap order while skipping/aborting conflicts, sequences+auto-renumbers migration-number collisions, auto-resolves pure-append hot-file conflicts by concatenation, and lands mechanically-mergeable overlap clusters without a re-conflict loop
preconditions: a planned batch of >=2 approved workspaces
behaviors.observable_outcome: clean members land; conflicting member skipped and its rebase aborted (worktree not left mid-rebase); migration collisions reported + renumbered before rebase; append-only cluster lands without strand; clusters converge idempotently

behaviors.statement: The Done⇔merged scanner detects a Done/AI-Reviewed issue whose branch isn't on base, NEVER reopens it, and forward-only auto-merges a clean ahead-only branch under rate limits; multiple false-positive guards (0-commit, too-far-behind, conflicting, has-merged-ws, deleted-branch) suppress unsafe action
behaviors.observable_outcome: real loss flagged + auto-merged (<=3/cycle, ahead>=1, behind<=20, conflict-free); 0-commit/too-behind/conflicting/deleted-branch are log-only with no status change; issue never reopened; idempotent after stamping mergedAt

behaviors.statement: Post-merge Done convergence is idempotent and crash-safe: mergedAt anchors recovery so a dropped HTTP response, a failed workspace-close, or a re-merge call all converge the issue to Done exactly once without rollback or double-transition
preconditions: git merge already landed (mergedAt set) OR branch is an ancestor
behaviors.observable_outcome: reconcileMergedIssue moves In-Review→Done, no-ops when already Done, preserves statusChangedAt; issue stays Done even when workspace close DB write fails; ancestor-branch reconciler closes ancestor In-Review ws and Dones it without a merge commit

behaviors.statement: Work stranded idle/In-Review/not-ready with commits and no review session is recovered by relaunching review (or marked ready when auto_review is off); the reconciler no-ops when disabled
preconditions: workspace idle, issue In Review, readyForMerge=false, has commits, no session, no prior review
behaviors.observable_outcome: a review session is (re)launched and the workspace leaves the stranded state; zero mutations when disabled via dep or DB pref

behaviors.statement: A configured verify/smoke pre-merge gate FAILS the merge when verify_script exits non-zero or a web project's boot/render smoke check fails; a configured gate that can't run (no worktree) fails closed; neither configured is a no-op pass
preconditions: verify_script_<id> set OR project stack profile isWeb
behaviors.observable_outcome: merge withheld on non-zero verify / failed smoke / missing-worktree-when-configured; passes when verify exits 0 and smoke renders; runs verify THEN smoke when both set

behaviors.statement: A no-own-dependency ticket with >=1 open dependent is classified as a foundational blocker eligible for synchronous merge, so dependents aren't cut from an empty pre-merge base
preconditions: workspace has no unresolved own dependency
behaviors.observable_outcome: isFoundationalBlocker true only when an open dependent exists AND own deps are Done; false for leaf tickets, unresolved-own-dep tickets, terminal-only dependents, non-blocking relations

permissions.notes: single-user local app — no RBAC; permission axis is near-trivial except the reviewer/system separation
reviewer may only SIGNAL via mark_ready_for_merge; self-merge is contractually forbidden so the system gates run (#822)

error_states.statement: merge of unapproved non-direct ws → 409 mergeReason=not_approved
conflicting merge → 409 mergeReason=conflict with conflictFiles, readyForMerge cleared
dirty main checkout → 409 mergeReason=dirty_main, no git merge
already-landed branch → 200 reconciled no-op, not an error
review on non-idle ws → 409 ReviewError (not a bare throw / 500)
stale server build → 503 reason=server_build_stale

external_integrations: git CLI (via shared git-service / git-exec adapter)

capabilities.name: review-merge

capabilities.business_purpose: Turns a kanban board's lifecycle into per-project DATA: a directed graph of stages (nodes) and legal moves (edges) with data-driven guards, attached agent skills, and cycle budgets — so 'what columns exist and how work moves' is configuration, not code. The same graph is simultaneously the board's column model, the AI agent's state machine, and the auto-routing policy. The legacy fixed board is just one built-in template (Simple Ticket).

actors.name: operator
actors.notes: single local user; edits templates in the visual builder, drags issues between columns, drives manual stage advances + spec-phase approvals from the UI

actors.name: agent
actors.notes: the per-ticket builder; advances the workflow via the MCP propose_transition tool, reports testsPassed signals

actors.name: fork-orchestrator
actors.notes: server workflow-fork.service — spawns/joins parallel children using the engine's structural placement primitives

entry_points.observable: returns project-scoped + global built-in templates (optionally with full node/edge graph)
create/update/delete/clone a non-builtin template; built-ins are immutable (400)
round-trips a template as a JSON envelope
previews which template/start node an issue would use
UI-driven manual stage advance; syncs issue status + writes spec phase artifact; 400 on illegal target
transition history, funnel/dwell/burndown analytics, per-stage workspace visits
agent advances its workspace along a legal/auto-routed edge; returns next stages for prompt re-injection
places a new workspace on the start node, derives issue status, records the initial system transition

entry_points.ref: syncCurrentNodeToStatus (on manual status move / move_issue / CLI drag)
entry_points.observable: repoints currentNodeId to the node mapping the new status, propagating to non-closed workspaces

behaviors.statement: An issue is matched to a workflow template by a 5-tier precedence: explicit id (project/global scope only) -> project default-for-type -> global default-for-type -> global Simple Ticket -> null (legacy status-only)
behaviors.observable_outcome: GET /resolve / resolveTemplateForIssue returns the expected templateId (e.g. bug->simple-bug, unmapped chore->simple-ticket); cross-scope explicit id is ignored

behaviors.statement: Creating a workspace places it on the template's start node, derives + persists the issue's status from that node, and records an initial 'Workspace started' system transition (fromNodeId null); no workflow => returns null
preconditions: issue resolves to a template with a start node
behaviors.observable_outcome: issue.currentNodeId = start node, issue.statusId = node's mapped status (e.g. In Progress), workspace.currentNodeId set, one system transition row inserted

behaviors.statement: Advancing a workspace along an existing outgoing edge records a transition, moves workspace.currentNodeId, and (unless a fork child) syncs the issue's currentNodeId + derived statusId/statusChangedAt
preconditions: workspace on a node with a legal outgoing edge to the target
behaviors.observable_outcome: result.ok true, toNode is the target, issue status derived from the new node (e.g. Review->In Review), transition history row appended

behaviors.statement: A transition to a node that is NOT an existing outgoing edge from the current node is refused (the graph IS the legality contract) — no teleporting to an arbitrary stage
preconditions: named/ided target has no edge from current node
behaviors.observable_outcome: result.ok false with 'No valid transition'; route returns 400; no issue/workspace mutation, no fork children materialized

behaviors.statement: With no explicit target, the engine auto-takes an edge IFF exactly one outgoing condition fires for the current signals; zero firing => refuse and ask the agent to choose; multiple firing => ambiguous, refuse
preconditions: current node has condition-gated outgoing edges; workspace signals computed
behaviors.observable_outcome: single fire: ok true, autoResolved true, lands on that node; zero: ok false 'Specify a target'; multiple: ok false 'Multiple edges fired'

behaviors.statement: An explicitly-chosen target whose condition evaluates to 'block' is refused even when a human/agent names it (e.g. can't advance to Review when tests demonstrably failed)
preconditions: explicit target edge condition evaluates block given signals
behaviors.observable_outcome: result.ok false with 'gated by condition'; no transition recorded

behaviors.statement: Entering a node already visited maxVisits times is refused, forcing human escalation — a runtime cycle guard against infinite agent looping
preconditions: target node has maxVisits>0 and visit count already at budget
behaviors.observable_outcome: result.ok false with 'visit budget'; node visit count not incremented past budget

behaviors.statement: The edge condition DSL maps a stored condition string to fire/block/manual: auto_on_exit_0 always fires; tests_pass/tests_fail key off reported flag (manual if unknown); diff_clean off file count; diff_touches:<glob> off a hand-rolled glob; unknown/agent_score/custom_js => manual
behaviors.observable_outcome: deterministic verdict per (condition, signals) pair driving auto-routing/gating

behaviors.statement: Saving a template enforces graph well-formedness: exactly one start, >=1 end, no orphan/dead-end nodes, all nodes reachable from start, unique node ids, unmarked cycles rejected (isLoop exempt), paired parallel-fork<->parallel-join
preconditions: a TemplateInput graph submitted to create/update
behaviors.observable_outcome: validateGraph returns specific human-readable error strings (empty = valid); create/update returns 400 + errors[] when malformed

behaviors.statement: When a human moves an issue's status (drag/move_issue/CLI), currentNodeId is repointed to a node whose statusName matches the new status and propagated to non-closed workspaces (closed workspaces untouched); no-op if no workflow or no matching node
preconditions: issue runs a workflow; new status maps to some node
behaviors.observable_outcome: issue.currentNodeId + non-closed workspaces' currentNodeId point at the matching node so the board column override stays consistent; closed workspaces unchanged

behaviors.statement: Creating an issue with a template attached keeps it in the column it was created in; currentNodeId aligns to the node mapping that status (start node only if the created status maps to a node, else null = workflow-inactive); a template not owned by the project is rejected BAD_REQUEST
behaviors.observable_outcome: Todo-created issue stays Todo with currentNodeId null; In Progress-created aligns to start node; foreign-project template => BAD_REQUEST

behaviors.statement: The visual builder can create/update/delete/clone/import/export project templates; built-in templates are immutable (edit/delete refused 'duplicate first'); create allows an empty-node draft
behaviors.observable_outcome: 201 on create with id; 400 on missing name/invalid graph; 400 on editing/deleting a built-in; valid round-trip via export->import

behaviors.statement: A parallel-fork spawns one child workspace+worktree+session per fork edge; once all (or all-surviving) children reach the join, the parent is structurally placed on the join node, the issue status syncs to the join, and children are marked joined + cleaned up — fork children never drive the shared issue's status
preconditions: workspace on a parallel-fork node of a parallel-review template
behaviors.observable_outcome: N children running with own currentNodeId; failed child launch tolerated; parent lands on join, issue status = join's status, children forkStatus joined + worktrees removed

behaviors.statement: The engine exposes per-workspace progress (transition history + next stages) and project analytics: per-node entered/advanced/dropoff funnel, average dwell-time trends, burndown, and per-stage workspace visits scoped to the project
behaviors.observable_outcome: GET /progress returns ordered transitions + valid next stages; /analytics returns funnel/durationTrends/burnDown; /analytics/:tpl/:node/workspaces lists visits with dwellMs + isCurrent

behaviors.statement: The agent-prompt block adapts to node role: a parallel-fork node tells the agent to STOP (server spawns branches); a spec-planning stage is human-gated ('Approve & continue', no propose_transition); a normal node injects the propose_transition contract + valid next stages + condition hints
behaviors.observable_outcome: rendered markdown contains/omits 'propose_transition' and the right next-stage list per node type

behaviors.statement: The MCP propose_transition tool is the AGENT's primary entry point to advance work — resolving the agent's workspace, applying the same legality/auto-route/gate/maxVisits rules, and returning the next stages for prompt re-injection
preconditions: agent in a workspace running a workflow
behaviors.observable_outcome: tool returns ok + next stages (or a refusal reason) and the board column advances; mirrors the REST transition contract

permissions.notes: single-user local app; the only enforced permission-like rule is built-in immutability, not RBAC

transitions.trigger: agent reaches fork; server spawns children
all surviving children joined; parent placed on join

error_states.statement: transition to a non-edge target -> ok false 'No valid transition' / route 400
no target + 0 or >1 firing conditions -> ok false (specify / multiple fired)
explicit target gated by an unsatisfied condition -> ok false 'gated by condition'
target node at maxVisits -> ok false 'visit budget', escalate
edit/delete of a built-in template -> 400 'cannot be edited/deleted'
createIssue with a template owned by another project -> BAD_REQUEST
malformed graph on save -> 400 + specific validateGraph error strings
git diff failure leaves diff signals undefined -> diff_* conditions degrade to 'manual' rather than crash
